1	# Add new provider
2	
3	## `@ai-sdk/<provider>` vs 3rd party package
4	
5	Every provider is welcome to create a 3rd party package. We are happy to link to it from our documentation.
6	
7	If you would prefer a 1st party `@ai-sdk/<provider>` package, please create an issue first to discuss.
8	
9	## Example
10	
11	https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8136/files
12	
13	## How
14	
15	1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
16	2. Set version in `packages/<provider>/package.json` to `0.0.0`
17	3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
18	4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts` depending on what model types the provider supports
19	5. Add documentation in `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/<last number + 10>-<provider>.mdx`
20	
21	See also [providers.md](providers.md)
22	
23	## When in pre-release mode
24	
25	If `main` is set up to publish `beta` releases, no further action is necessary. Just make sure not to backport it to the `vX.Y` stable branch since it will result in an npm version conflict once we exit pre-release mode on `main`
26

1 # Provider Architecture 2 3 The AI SDK uses a layered provider architecture that follows the adapter pattern, enabling support for multiple AI providers through a unified interface. 4 5 ## Architecture Overview 6 7 ```mermaid 8 graph LR 9 AI["Main Package
ai"] --> PROVIDER["Specifications
@ai-sdk/provider"] 10 AI --> UTILS["Shared Utilities
@ai-sdk/provider-utils"] 11 UTILS --> PROVIDER 12 OPENAI["Provider
e.g. @ai-sdk/openai"] --> PROVIDER 13 OPENAI --> UTILS 14 OPENAI --> OPENAI_API["Provider API
e.g. OpenAI API
(External)"] 15 16 classDef core fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#4a148c,stroke-width:2px 17 classDef spec fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#e65100,stroke-width:2px 18 classDef utils fill:#e8f5e8,stroke:#1b5e20,stroke-width:2px 19 classDef provider fill:#fff8e1,stroke:#ff6f00,stroke-width:2px 20 classDef external fill:#f1f8e9,stroke:#33691e,stroke-width:2px 21 22 class AI core 23 class PROVIDER spec 24 class UTILS utils 25 class OPENAI provider 26 class OPENAI_API external 27 ``` 28 29 See the [provider abstraction architecture doc](../architecture/provider-abstraction.md) for a more in-depth guide. 30

1	import { JSONArray, JSONValue } from '../../json-value';
2	import { ImageModelV2CallOptions } from './image-model-v2-call-options';
3	import { ImageModelV2CallWarning } from './image-model-v2-call-warning';
4	
5	export type ImageModelV2ProviderMetadata = Record<
6	  string,
7	  {
8	    images: JSONArray;
9	  } & JSONValue
10	>;
11	
12	type GetMaxImagesPerCallFunction = (options: {
13	  modelId: string;
14	}) => PromiseLike<number | undefined> | number | undefined;
15	
16	/**
17	 * Image generation model specification version 2.
18	 */
19	export type ImageModelV2 = {
20	  /**
21	   * The image model must specify which image model interface
22	   * version it implements. This will allow us to evolve the image
23	   * model interface and retain backwards compatibility. The different
24	   * implementation versions can be handled as a discriminated union
25	   * on our side.
26	   */
27	  readonly specificationVersion: 'v2';
28	
29	  /**
30	   * Name of the provider for logging purposes.
31	   */
32	  readonly provider: string;
33	
34	  /**
35	   * Provider-specific model ID for logging purposes.
36	   */
37	  readonly modelId: string;
38	
39	  /**
40	   * Limit of how many images can be generated in a single API call.
41	   * Can be set to a number for a fixed limit, to undefined to use
42	   * the global limit, or a function that returns a number or undefined,
43	   * optionally as a promise.
44	   */
45	  readonly maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | GetMaxImagesPerCallFunction;
46	
47	  /**
48	   * Generates an array of images.
49	   */
50	  doGenerate(options: ImageModelV2CallOptions): PromiseLike<{
51	    /**
52	     * Generated images as base64 encoded strings or binary data.
53	     * The images should be returned without any unnecessary conversion.
54	     * If the API returns base64 encoded strings, the images should be returned
55	     * as base64 encoded strings. If the API returns binary data, the images should
56	     * be returned as binary data.
57	     */
58	    images: Array<string> | Array<Uint8Array>;
59	
60	    /**
61	     * Warnings for the call, e.g. unsupported settings.
62	     */
63	    warnings: Array<ImageModelV2CallWarning>;
64	
65	    /**
66	     * Additional provider-specific metadata. They are passed through
67	     * from the provider to the AI SDK and enable provider-specific
68	     * results that can be fully encapsulated in the provider.
69	     *
70	     * The outer record is keyed by the provider name, and the inner
71	     * record is provider-specific metadata. It always includes an
72	     * `images` key with image-specific metadata
73	     *
74	     * ```ts
75	     * {
76	     * "openai": {
77	     * "images": ["revisedPrompt": "Revised prompt here."]
78	     * }
79	     * }
80	     * ```
81	     */
82	    providerMetadata?: ImageModelV2ProviderMetadata;
83	
84	    /**
85	     * Response information for telemetry and debugging purposes.
86	     */
87	    response: {
88	      /**
89	       * Timestamp for the start of the generated response.
90	       */
91	      timestamp: Date;
92	
93	      /**
94	       * The ID of the response model that was used to generate the response.
95	       */
96	      modelId: string;
97	
98	      /**
99	       * Response headers.
100	       */
101	      headers: Record<string, string> | undefined;
102	    };
103	  }>;
104	};
105

12	   */

17	   * Must have the format `{width}x{height}`.
18	   * `undefined` will use the provider's default size.
19	   */
20	  size: `${number}x${number}` | undefined;
21	
22	  /**
23	   * Aspect ratio of the images to generate.
24	   * Must have the format `{width}:{height}`.
25	   * `undefined` will use the provider's default aspect ratio.
26	   */
27	  aspectRatio: `${number}:${number}` | undefined;
28	
29	  /**
30	   * Seed for the image generation.
31	   * `undefined` will use the provider's default seed.
32	   */
33	  seed: number | undefined;
34	
35	  /**
36	   * Array of images for image editing or variation generation.
37	   * The images should be provided as base64 encoded strings or binary data.
38	   */
39	  files: ImageModelV3File[] | undefined;
40	
41	  /**
42	   * Mask image for inpainting operations.
43	   * The mask should be provided as base64 encoded strings or binary data.
44	   */
45	  mask: ImageModelV3File | undefined;
46	
47	  /**
48	   * Additional provider-specific options that are passed through to the provider
49	   * as body parameters.
50	   *
51	   * The outer record is keyed by the provider name, and the inner
52	   * record is keyed by the provider-specific metadata key.
53	   *
54	   * ```ts
55	   * {
56	   *   "openai": {
57	   *     "style": "vivid"
58	   *   }
59	   * }
60	   * ```
61	   */
62	  providerOptions: SharedV3ProviderOptions;
63	
64	  /**
65	   * Abort signal for cancelling the operation.
66	   */
67	  abortSignal?: AbortSignal;
68	
69	  /**

71	   * Only applicable for HTTP-based providers.
72	   */
73	  headers?: Record<string, string | undefined>;

4	// minimal version of the schema, focused on what is needed for the implementation
5	// this approach limits breakages when the API changes and increases efficiency
6	export const openaiImageResponseSchema = lazySchema(() =>
7	  zodSchema(
8	    z.object({
9	      created: z.number().nullish(),
10	      data: z.array(
11	        z.object({
12	          b64_json: z.string(),
13	          revised_prompt: z.string().nullish(),
14	        }),
15	      ),
16	      background: z.string().nullish(),
17	      output_format: z.string().nullish(),
18	      size: z.string().nullish(),
19	      quality: z.string().nullish(),
20	      usage: z
21	        .object({
22	          input_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
23	          output_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
24	          total_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
25	          input_tokens_details: z
26	            .object({
27	              image_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
28	              text_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
29	            })
30	            .nullish(),
31	        })
32	        .nullish(),
33	    }),
34	  ),
35	);

1	# Provider Development Notes
2	
3	## Provider Options Schemas
4	
5	Provider options schemas are user facing.
6	We want them to be as restrictive as possible, so that we have more flexibility with future changes and allow for meaningful `null` values.
7	
8	- use `.optional()` unless `null` is meaningful
9	
10	## Response Schemas
11	
12	Response schemas need to be flexible enough to deal with provider API changes that do not affect our processing
13	to prevent unnecessary breakages.
14	
15	- keep them minimal (no unused properties)
16	- use `.nullish()` instead of `.optional()`
17	
18	## Provider-Specific Model Options Types
19	
20	Types and Zod schemas for the provider specific model options follow the pattern `{Provider}{ModelType}Options`, e.g. `AnthropicLanguageModelOptions`.
21	If a provider has multiple implementations for the same model type, add a qualifier: `{Provider}{ModelType}{Qualifier}Options`, e.g. `OpenAILanguageModelChatOptions` and `OpenAILanguageModelResponsesOptions`.
22	If options apply provider-wide rather than to a specific model type, use `{Provider}ProviderOptions` instead, e.g. `GatewayProviderOptions`.
23	
24	- types are PascalCase, Zod schemas are camelCase (e.g. `openaiLanguageModelChatOptions`)
25	- types must be exported from the provider package, Zod schemas must not
26	
27	## Provider Method Names
28	
29	For the Provider v3 interface, we require fully specified names with a "Model" suffix, e.g. `languageModel(id)` or `imageModel(id)`. These help with clarity for both developers and agents.

54	     * The images should be returned without any unnecessary conversion.
55	     * If the API returns base64 encoded strings, the images should be returned

4	// minimal version of the schema, focused on what is needed for the implementation
5	// this approach limits breakages when the API changes and increases efficiency
6	export const openaiImageResponseSchema = lazySchema(() =>
7	  zodSchema(
8	    z.object({
9	      created: z.number().nullish(),
10	      data: z.array(
11	        z.object({
12	          b64_json: z.string(),

14	        }),
15	      ),
16	      background: z.string().nullish(),
17	      output_format: z.string().nullish(),
18	      size: z.string().nullish(),
19	      quality: z.string().nullish(),
20	      usage: z
21	        .object({
22	          input_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
23	          output_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
24	          total_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
25	          input_tokens_details: z
26	            .object({
27	              image_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
28	              text_tokens: z.number().nullish(),
29	            })
30	            .nullish(),
31	        })
32	        .nullish(),
33	    }),
34	  ),

1	# AI SDK - Black Forest Labs Provider
2	
3	The **[Black Forest Labs provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/black-forest-labs)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs) adds image model support for the [Black Forest Labs API](https://docs.bfl.ai/).
4	
5	## Setup
6	
7	The Black Forest Labs provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs` module. You can install it with
8	
9	```bash
10	pnpm add @ai-sdk/black-forest-labs
11	```
12	
13	## Skill for Coding Agents
14	
15	If you use coding agents such as Claude Code or Cursor, we highly recommend adding the AI SDK skill to your repository:
16	
17	```shell
18	npx skills add vercel/ai
19	```
20	
21	## Provider Instance
22	
23	You can import the default provider instance `blackForestLabs` from `@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs`:
24	
25	```ts
26	import { blackForestLabs } from '@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs';
27	```
28	
29	## Image Generation Example
30	
31	```ts
32	import fs from 'node:fs';
33	import { blackForestLabs } from '@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs';
34	import { generateImage } from 'ai';
35	
36	const { image } = await generateImage({
37	  model: blackForestLabs.image('flux-pro-1.1'),
38	  prompt: 'A cat wearing a intricate robe',
39	});
40	
41	const filename = `image-${Date.now()}.png`;
42	fs.writeFileSync(filename, image.uint8Array);
43	console.log(`Image saved to ${filename}`);
44	```
45	
46	## Additional Options
47	
48	If you want to pass additional inputs to the model besides the prompt, use the `providerOptions.blackForestLabs` property:
49	
50	```ts
51	import {
52	  blackForestLabs,
53	  type BlackForestLabsImageProviderOptions,
54	} from '@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs';
55	import { generateImage } from 'ai';
56	
57	const { image } = await generateImage({
58	  model: blackForestLabs.image('flux-pro-1.1'),
59	  prompt: 'A cat wearing an intricate robe',
60	  aspectRatio: '16:9',
61	  providerOptions: {
62	    blackForestLabs: {
63	      outputFormat: 'png',
64	    } satisfies BlackForestLabsImageProviderOptions,
65	  },
66	});
67	```
68	
69	## Configuring Base URL
70	
71	By default, the provider uses `https://api.bfl.ai/v1`. You can override this to use regional or legacy endpoints:
72	
73	```ts
74	import { createBlackForestLabs } from '@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs';
75	
76	const blackForestLabs = createBlackForestLabs({
77	  baseURL: 'https://api.eu.bfl.ai/v1',
78	  apiKey: process.env.BFL_API_KEY,
79	});
80	```
81	
82	## Configuring Polling
83	
84	You can customize how often the client polls for image completion and how long it waits before timing out:
85	
86	```ts
87	import { createBlackForestLabs } from '@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs';
88	
89	const blackForestLabs = createBlackForestLabs({
90	  apiKey: process.env.BFL_API_KEY,
91	  // Poll every 500ms, timeout after 5 minutes
92	  pollIntervalMillis: 500,
93	  pollTimeoutMillis: 5 * 60_000,
94	});
95	```
96	
97	You can also override these polling settings per request via `providerOptions.blackForestLabs`:
98	
99	```ts
100	import { blackForestLabs } from '@ai-sdk/black-forest-labs';
101	import { generateImage } from 'ai';
102	
103	const { image } = await generateImage({
104	  model: blackForestLabs.image('flux-pro-1.1'),
105	  prompt: 'A cat wearing an intricate robe',
106	  providerOptions: {
107	    blackForestLabs: {
108	      pollIntervalMillis: 250,
109	      pollTimeoutMillis: 30_000,
110	    },
111	  },
112	});
113	```
114	
115	## Documentation
116	
117	See the [Black Forest Labs provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/black-forest-labs) for more information.
118

# AI SDK - OpenAI Provider

The **[OpenAI provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/openai)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs)
contains language model support for the OpenAI chat and completion APIs and embedding model support for the OpenAI embeddings API.

## Setup

The OpenAI provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/openai` module. You can install it with

```bash
npm i @ai-sdk/openai
```

## Skill for Coding Agents

If you use coding agents such as Claude Code or Cursor, we highly recommend adding the AI SDK skill to your repository:

```shell
npx skills add vercel/ai
```

## Provider Instance

You can import the default provider instance `openai` from `@ai-sdk/openai`:

```ts
import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';
```

## Example

```ts
import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';
import { generateText } from 'ai';

const { text } = await generateText({
  model: openai('gpt-5-mini'),
  prompt: 'Write a vegetarian lasagna recipe for 4 people.',
});
```

## Documentation

Please check out the **[OpenAI provider documentation](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/openai)** for more information.

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary report:

## Comprehensive Analysis: AI Provider Package Structure in Vercel AI SDK

Based on my thorough exploration of the monorepo, here's a complete understanding of the AI provider package architecture:

---

### 1. **OpenAI Provider Package Structure** (`packages/openai/`)

#### Directory Layout:
```
packages/openai/
├── src/
│   ├── chat/              (Chat-based language models)
│   ├── completion/        (Completion API models)
│   ├── embedding/         (Text embedding models)
│   ├── image/             (Image generation models)
│   ├── speech/            (Text-to-speech)
│   ├── transcription/     (Speech-to-text)
│   ├── responses/         (OpenAI Responses API)
│   ├── tool/              (Tool definitions)
│   ├── internal/
│   ├── openai-provider.ts (Main provider factory)
│   ├── openai-config.ts
│   ├── openai-error.ts
│   ├── openai-tools.ts
│   ├── openai-language-model-capabilities.ts
│   ├── index.ts           (Public exports)
│   └── version.ts
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── tsup.config.ts
├── vitest.node.config.js
├── vitest.edge.config.js
└── turbo.json
```

#### Key Files Examined:
- **Absolute Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/openai/`

**package.json** Details:
- Version: 3.0.50
- License: Apache-2.0
- Main entry points: `./dist/index.js` (CJS), `./dist/index.mjs` (ESM)
- Two export conditions: main package and `./internal` for internal-only exports
- Dependencies: `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace references)
- Peer dependencies: `zod` (3.25.76 || 4.1.8)
- Scripts include: build, type-check, test:node, test:edge
- Node requirement: >=18

**tsconfig.json** Structure:
- Extends: `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- References composite projects: `provider` and `provider-utils`
- Output: compiled to `dist/`

#### Provider Factory Pattern (`openai-provider.ts`):

**Exports:**
```typescript
- createOpenAI(options?: OpenAIProviderSettings): OpenAIProvider
- openai: default OpenAI provider instance
```

**OpenAIProvider Interface** supports 8+ methods:
1. `languageModel(modelId)` - default call handler
2. `chat(modelId)` - chat completions
3. `responses(modelId)` - responses API
4. `completion(modelId)` - completion API
5. `embedding(modelId)` / `embeddingModel(modelId)` - text embeddings
6. `image(modelId)` / `imageModel(modelId)` - **image generation**
7. `transcription(modelId)` - speech-to-text
8. `speech(modelId)` - text-to-speech

**OpenAIProviderSettings:**
- `apiKey` - from env or parameter
- `baseURL` - defaults to `https://api.openai.com/v1`
- `organization` - OpenAI org setting
- `project` - OpenAI project setting
- `headers` - custom headers
- `name` - provider name override (for 3rd-party providers)
- `fetch` - custom fetch implementation

#### Image Generation Implementation:

**Model Class:** `OpenAIImageModel` (implements `ImageModelV3`)
- Specification version: v3
- Located: `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.ts`
- Supports models: `dall-e-3`, `dall-e-2`, `gpt-image-1`, `gpt-image-1-mini`, `gpt-image-1.5`, `chatgpt-image-latest`
- Max images per call varies by model (1-10)

**Key Methods:**
- `doGenerate()` - handles both image generation and editing
- Supports two API endpoints:
  - `/images/generations` - text-to-image generation
  - `/images/edits` - image editing with files and masks
- Returns images as base64-encoded strings
- Includes usage tracking (input/output/total tokens)

**Output Structure:**
```typescript
{
  images: string[] | Uint8Array[],
  warnings: SharedV3Warning[],
  usage?: { inputTokens?, outputTokens?, totalTokens? },
  response: { timestamp, modelId, headers },
  providerMetadata?: { openai: { images: [...] } }
}
```

---

### 2. **Image-Only Provider Example: Black Forest Labs** (`packages/black-forest-labs/`)

This is a **simpler, image-only provider** - ideal reference for minimal image generation setup.

#### Directory Layout:
```
packages/black-forest-labs/
├── src/
│   ├── black-forest-labs-provider.ts
│   ├── black-forest-labs-image-model.ts
│   ├── black-forest-labs-image-settings.ts
│   ├── index.ts
│   ├── version.ts
│   └── (test files)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── (build config files)
```

**Absolute Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/black-forest-labs/`

**package.json:**
- Version: 1.0.27
- Minimal dependencies (only `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`)
- Same export pattern as OpenAI

**Provider Interface** (simpler!):
```typescript
BlackForestLabsProvider {
  image(modelId): ImageModelV3
  imageModel(modelId): ImageModelV3
  // Throws NoSuchModelError for unsupported capabilities:
  languageModel()
  embeddingModel()
}
```

**Provider Settings:**
- `apiKey` - from `BFL_API_KEY` env
- `baseURL` - defaults to `https://api.bfl.ai/v1`
- `headers` - custom headers
- `fetch` - custom fetch
- `pollIntervalMillis` - 500ms default (async polling)
- `pollTimeoutMillis` - 60000ms default

**Factory Function Pattern:**
```typescript
createBlackForestLabs(options): BlackForestLabsProvider
blackForestLabs: default instance
```

**Supported Models:**
- `flux-kontext-pro`
- `flux-kontext-max`
- `flux-pro-1.1-ultra`
- `flux-pro-1.1`
- `flux-pro-1.0-fill`

**Image Model Key Differences:**
- Single image per call (`maxImagesPerCall = 1`)
- Uses async polling for results (not immediate response)
- Supports `aspectRatio` instead of fixed sizes
- Includes output format control (PNG, etc.)

---

### 3. **Core Provider Architecture** (`packages/provider/`)

The foundational types and interfaces all providers implement.

#### Structure:
```
packages/provider/src/
├── provider/
│   ├── v2/ (legacy)
│   ├── v3/ → provider-v3.ts (current spec)
│   └── index.ts
├── image-model/
│   ├── v3/ → image-model-v3.ts (current)
│   ├── image-model-v3-call-options.ts
│   ├── image-model-v3-file.ts
│   ├── image-model-v3-usage.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── language-model/  (text generation)
├── embedding-model/ (text embeddings)
├── speech-model/    (text-to-speech)
├── transcription-model/ (speech-to-text)
├── video-model/     (video generation)
├── shared/          (common types)
├── errors/          (error definitions)
└── index.ts
```

**Absolute Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/provider/`

#### ProviderV3 Interface:
```typescript
interface ProviderV3 {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3'
  
  languageModel(modelId: string): LanguageModelV3
  embeddingModel(modelId: string): EmbeddingModelV3
  imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3        // Image generation
  transcriptionModel?(modelId: string): TranscriptionModelV3
  speechModel?(modelId: string): SpeechModelV3
  rerankingModel?(modelId: string): RerankingModelV3
}
```

#### ImageModelV3 Interface:
All image models must implement this:

```typescript
type ImageModelV3 = {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3'
  readonly provider: string                         // e.g., "openai.image"
  readonly modelId: string                          // Model identifier
  readonly maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | Function
  
  doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions): Promise<{
    images: (string | Uint8Array)[],              // Base64 or binary
    warnings: SharedV3Warning[],
    providerMetadata?: ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata,
    response: {
      timestamp: Date
      modelId: string
      headers: Record<string, string> | undefined
    }
    usage?: {
      inputTokens?: number
      outputTokens?: number
      totalTokens?: number
    }
  }>
}
```

#### ImageModelV3CallOptions:
```typescript
type ImageModelV3CallOptions = {
  prompt: string | undefined
  n: number                                         // Number of images
  size: `${number}x${number}` | undefined          // e.g., "1024x1024"
  aspectRatio: `${number}:${number}` | undefined   // e.g., "16:9"
  seed: number | undefined
  files: ImageModelV3File[] | undefined            // For editing
  mask: ImageModelV3File | undefined               // For inpainting
  providerOptions: SharedV3ProviderOptions         // Provider-specific opts
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal
  headers?: Record<string, string | undefined>
}
```

---

### 4. **Provider-Utils Package** (`packages/provider-utils/`)

Shared utilities for all providers.

#### Key Utilities for Image Generation:
- `postJsonToApi()` - POST JSON requests
- `postFormDataToApi()` - POST form data (image uploads)
- `createJsonResponseHandler()` - Parse and validate JSON responses
- `convertBase64ToUint8Array()` - Convert base64 to binary
- `convertToFormData()` - Build form data from objects
- `downloadBlob()` - Fetch images from URLs
- `loadApiKey()` - Load API key from env/options
- `combineHeaders()` - Merge header objects
- Schema validation: `lazySchema()`, `zodSchema()`

---

### 5. **Other Image Generation Providers**

The monorepo contains these additional image providers:

| Provider | Location | Scope | Key Features |
|----------|----------|-------|--------------|
| **Replicate** | `packages/replicate/` | Image + Video | Models hosted on Replicate, async polling |
| **FAL** | `packages/fal/` | Image + Video + Speech + Transcription | Multiple media types, async polling |
| **Prodia** | `packages/prodia/` | Image + Language + Video | Multi-capability provider |
| **Luma** | `packages/luma/` | Video generation | Video-specific (no image) |

---

### 6. **Export Patterns**

Both OpenAI and Black Forest Labs follow consistent export patterns:

**Main Entry (`index.ts`):**
```typescript
export {
  createProviderName,      // Factory function
  providerName,            // Default instance
} from './provider-file'

export type {
  ProviderNameProvider,
  ProviderNameProviderSettings,
  ModelNameOptions,
  // ... specific capability options
}

export { VERSION }
```

**Usage:**
```typescript
import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai'
import { generateImage } from 'ai'

const { image } = await generateImage({
  model: openai.image('dall-e-3'),
  prompt: 'A cat'
})
```

---

### 7. **Build & TypeScript Configuration**

**All providers use identical build setup:**

- **tsconfig.json**: Extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- **tsup.config.ts**: Bundles ESM and CJS outputs
- **Build command**: `tsup --tsconfig tsconfig.build.json`
- **Test framework**: vitest (separate node and edge configs)
- **Output structure**: 
  - `dist/index.js` (CommonJS)
  - `dist/index.mjs` (ES Module)
  - `dist/index.d.ts` (TypeScript declarations)

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | OpenAI | Black Forest Labs | Core |
|--------|--------|------------------|------|
| **Scope** | Multi-capability | Image-only | Framework |
| **Models Supported** | 8+ categories | 1 category | N/A |
| **Provider Interface** | 8 methods | 2 methods | ProviderV3 |
| **Image Model Class** | OpenAIImageModel | BlackForestLabsImageModel | ImageModelV3 |
| **Async Polling** | No | Yes (500ms intervals) | Optional |
| **API Response Format** | JSON (base64 images) | JSON (async results) | Flexible |
| **Max Images/Call** | 1-10 (model dependent) | 1 | Defined per model |
| **Complexity** | High (8 capabilities) | Low (image-only) | Minimal |

This architecture demonstrates a flexible, extensible pattern where:
1. Core interfaces (`ProviderV3`, `ImageModelV3`) define contracts
2. Providers implement these interfaces with specific capabilities
3. Factory functions create instances with configuration
4. Shared utilities reduce duplication
5. Providers can be simple (image-only) or complex (multi-capability)

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary report:

## Comprehensive Analysis: AI Provider Package Structure in Vercel AI SDK

Based on my thorough exploration of the monorepo, here's a complete understanding of the AI provider package architecture:

---

### 1. **OpenAI Provider Package Structure** (`packages/openai/`)

#### Directory Layout:
```
packages/openai/
├── src/
│   ├── chat/              (Chat-based language models)
│   ├── completion/        (Completion API models)
│   ├── embedding/         (Text embedding models)
│   ├── image/             (Image generation models)
│   ├── speech/            (Text-to-speech)
│   ├── transcription/     (Speech-to-text)
│   ├── responses/         (OpenAI Responses API)
│   ├── tool/              (Tool definitions)
│   ├── internal/
│   ├── openai-provider.ts (Main provider factory)
│   ├── openai-config.ts
│   ├── openai-error.ts
│   ├── openai-tools.ts
│   ├── openai-language-model-capabilities.ts
│   ├── index.ts           (Public exports)
│   └── version.ts
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── tsup.config.ts
├── vitest.node.config.js
├── vitest.edge.config.js
└── turbo.json
```

#### Key Files Examined:
- **Absolute Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/openai/`

**package.json** Details:
- Version: 3.0.50
- License: Apache-2.0
- Main entry points: `./dist/index.js` (CJS), `./dist/index.mjs` (ESM)
- Two export conditions: main package and `./internal` for internal-only exports
- Dependencies: `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace references)
- Peer dependencies: `zod` (3.25.76 || 4.1.8)
- Scripts include: build, type-check, test:node, test:edge
- Node requirement: >=18

**tsconfig.json** Structure:
- Extends: `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- References composite projects: `provider` and `provider-utils`
- Output: compiled to `dist/`

#### Provider Factory Pattern (`openai-provider.ts`):

**Exports:**
```typescript
- createOpenAI(options?: OpenAIProviderSettings): OpenAIProvider
- openai: default OpenAI provider instance
```

**OpenAIProvider Interface** supports 8+ methods:
1. `languageModel(modelId)` - default call handler
2. `chat(modelId)` - chat completions
3. `responses(modelId)` - responses API
4. `completion(modelId)` - completion API
5. `embedding(modelId)` / `embeddingModel(modelId)` - text embeddings
6. `image(modelId)` / `imageModel(modelId)` - **image generation**
7. `transcription(modelId)` - speech-to-text
8. `speech(modelId)` - text-to-speech

**OpenAIProviderSettings:**
- `apiKey` - from env or parameter
- `baseURL` - defaults to `https://api.openai.com/v1`
- `organization` - OpenAI org setting
- `project` - OpenAI project setting
- `headers` - custom headers
- `name` - provider name override (for 3rd-party providers)
- `fetch` - custom fetch implementation

#### Image Generation Implementation:

**Model Class:** `OpenAIImageModel` (implements `ImageModelV3`)
- Specification version: v3
- Located: `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.ts`
- Supports models: `dall-e-3`, `dall-e-2`, `gpt-image-1`, `gpt-image-1-mini`, `gpt-image-1.5`, `chatgpt-image-latest`
- Max images per call varies by model (1-10)

**Key Methods:**
- `doGenerate()` - handles both image generation and editing
- Supports two API endpoints:
  - `/images/generations` - text-to-image generation
  - `/images/edits` - image editing with files and masks
- Returns images as base64-encoded strings
- Includes usage tracking (input/output/total tokens)

**Output Structure:**
```typescript
{
  images: string[] | Uint8Array[],
  warnings: SharedV3Warning[],
  usage?: { inputTokens?, outputTokens?, totalTokens? },
  response: { timestamp, modelId, headers },
  providerMetadata?: { openai: { images: [...] } }
}
```

---

### 2. **Image-Only Provider Example: Black Forest Labs** (`packages/black-forest-labs/`)

This is a **simpler, image-only provider** - ideal reference for minimal image generation setup.

#### Directory Layout:
```
packages/black-forest-labs/
├── src/
│   ├── black-forest-labs-provider.ts
│   ├── black-forest-labs-image-model.ts
│   ├── black-forest-labs-image-settings.ts
│   ├── index.ts
│   ├── version.ts
│   └── (test files)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── (build config files)
```

**Absolute Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/black-forest-labs/`

**package.json:**
- Version: 1.0.27
- Minimal dependencies (only `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`)
- Same export pattern as OpenAI

**Provider Interface** (simpler!):
```typescript
BlackForestLabsProvider {
  image(modelId): ImageModelV3
  imageModel(modelId): ImageModelV3
  // Throws NoSuchModelError for unsupported capabilities:
  languageModel()
  embeddingModel()
}
```

**Provider Settings:**
- `apiKey` - from `BFL_API_KEY` env
- `baseURL` - defaults to `https://api.bfl.ai/v1`
- `headers` - custom headers
- `fetch` - custom fetch
- `pollIntervalMillis` - 500ms default (async polling)
- `pollTimeoutMillis` - 60000ms default

**Factory Function Pattern:**
```typescript
createBlackForestLabs(options): BlackForestLabsProvider
blackForestLabs: default instance
```

**Supported Models:**
- `flux-kontext-pro`
- `flux-kontext-max`
- `flux-pro-1.1-ultra`
- `flux-pro-1.1`
- `flux-pro-1.0-fill`

**Image Model Key Differences:**
- Single image per call (`maxImagesPerCall = 1`)
- Uses async polling for results (not immediate response)
- Supports `aspectRatio` instead of fixed sizes
- Includes output format control (PNG, etc.)

---

### 3. **Core Provider Architecture** (`packages/provider/`)

The foundational types and interfaces all providers implement.

#### Structure:
```
packages/provider/src/
├── provider/
│   ├── v2/ (legacy)
│   ├── v3/ → provider-v3.ts (current spec)
│   └── index.ts
├── image-model/
│   ├── v3/ → image-model-v3.ts (current)
│   ├── image-model-v3-call-options.ts
│   ├── image-model-v3-file.ts
│   ├── image-model-v3-usage.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── language-model/  (text generation)
├── embedding-model/ (text embeddings)
├── speech-model/    (text-to-speech)
├── transcription-model/ (speech-to-text)
├── video-model/     (video generation)
├── shared/          (common types)
├── errors/          (error definitions)
└── index.ts
```

**Absolute Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/provider/`

#### ProviderV3 Interface:
```typescript
interface ProviderV3 {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3'
  
  languageModel(modelId: string): LanguageModelV3
  embeddingModel(modelId: string): EmbeddingModelV3
  imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3        // Image generation
  transcriptionModel?(modelId: string): TranscriptionModelV3
  speechModel?(modelId: string): SpeechModelV3
  rerankingModel?(modelId: string): RerankingModelV3
}
```

#### ImageModelV3 Interface:
All image models must implement this:

```typescript
type ImageModelV3 = {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3'
  readonly provider: string                         // e.g., "openai.image"
  readonly modelId: string                          // Model identifier
  readonly maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | Function
  
  doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions): Promise<{
    images: (string | Uint8Array)[],              // Base64 or binary
    warnings: SharedV3Warning[],
    providerMetadata?: ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata,
    response: {
      timestamp: Date
      modelId: string
      headers: Record<string, string> | undefined
    }
    usage?: {
      inputTokens?: number
      outputTokens?: number
      totalTokens?: number
    }
  }>
}
```

#### ImageModelV3CallOptions:
```typescript
type ImageModelV3CallOptions = {
  prompt: string | undefined
  n: number                                         // Number of images
  size: `${number}x${number}` | undefined          // e.g., "1024x1024"
  aspectRatio: `${number}:${number}` | undefined   // e.g., "16:9"
  seed: number | undefined
  files: ImageModelV3File[] | undefined            // For editing
  mask: ImageModelV3File | undefined               // For inpainting
  providerOptions: SharedV3ProviderOptions         // Provider-specific opts
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal
  headers?: Record<string, string | undefined>
}
```

---

### 4. **Provider-Utils Package** (`packages/provider-utils/`)

Shared utilities for all providers.

#### Key Utilities for Image Generation:
- `postJsonToApi()` - POST JSON requests
- `postFormDataToApi()` - POST form data (image uploads)
- `createJsonResponseHandler()` - Parse and validate JSON responses
- `convertBase64ToUint8Array()` - Convert base64 to binary
- `convertToFormData()` - Build form data from objects
- `downloadBlob()` - Fetch images from URLs
- `loadApiKey()` - Load API key from env/options
- `combineHeaders()` - Merge header objects
- Schema validation: `lazySchema()`, `zodSchema()`

---

### 5. **Other Image Generation Providers**

The monorepo contains these additional image providers:

| Provider | Location | Scope | Key Features |
|----------|----------|-------|--------------|
| **Replicate** | `packages/replicate/` | Image + Video | Models hosted on Replicate, async polling |
| **FAL** | `packages/fal/` | Image + Video + Speech + Transcription | Multiple media types, async polling |
| **Prodia** | `packages/prodia/` | Image + Language + Video | Multi-capability provider |
| **Luma** | `packages/luma/` | Video generation | Video-specific (no image) |

---

### 6. **Export Patterns**

Both OpenAI and Black Forest Labs follow consistent export patterns:

**Main Entry (`index.ts`):**
```typescript
export {
  createProviderName,      // Factory function
  providerName,            // Default instance
} from './provider-file'

export type {
  ProviderNameProvider,
  ProviderNameProviderSettings,
  ModelNameOptions,
  // ... specific capability options
}

export { VERSION }
```

**Usage:**
```typescript
import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai'
import { generateImage } from 'ai'

const { image } = await generateImage({
  model: openai.image('dall-e-3'),
  prompt: 'A cat'
})
```

---

### 7. **Build & TypeScript Configuration**

**All providers use identical build setup:**

- **tsconfig.json**: Extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- **tsup.config.ts**: Bundles ESM and CJS outputs
- **Build command**: `tsup --tsconfig tsconfig.build.json`
- **Test framework**: vitest (separate node and edge configs)
- **Output structure**: 
  - `dist/index.js` (CommonJS)
  - `dist/index.mjs` (ES Module)
  - `dist/index.d.ts` (TypeScript declarations)

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | OpenAI | Black Forest Labs | Core |
|--------|--------|------------------|------|
| **Scope** | Multi-capability | Image-only | Framework |
| **Models Supported** | 8+ categories | 1 category | N/A |
| **Provider Interface** | 8 methods | 2 methods | ProviderV3 |
| **Image Model Class** | OpenAIImageModel | BlackForestLabsImageModel | ImageModelV3 |
| **Async Polling** | No | Yes (500ms intervals) | Optional |
| **API Response Format** | JSON (base64 images) | JSON (async results) | Flexible |
| **Max Images/Call** | 1-10 (model dependent) | 1 | Defined per model |
| **Complexity** | High (8 capabilities) | Low (image-only) | Minimal |

This architecture demonstrates a flexible, extensible pattern where:
1. Core interfaces (`ProviderV3`, `ImageModelV3`) define contracts
2. Providers implement these interfaces with specific capabilities
3. Factory functions create instances with configuration
4. Shared utilities reduce duplication
5. Providers can be simple (image-only) or complex (multi-capability)

## Summary: Testing Patterns, Build Configuration, and Package Setup in Vercel AI SDK Monorepo

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. Here are the comprehensive findings:

---

### 1. IMAGE MODEL TESTING PATTERNS

**Test Structure & Location:**
- Test files are co-located with source files: `*-image-model.test.ts` (e.g., `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.test.ts`)
- Tests use **Vitest** with a test server powered by `@ai-sdk/test-server`
- Test fixtures stored in `__fixtures__` subdirectories (e.g., `/packages/openai/src/image/__fixtures__/`)

**Test Server Setup (from `packages/test-server/src/with-vitest.ts`):**
```typescript
const server = createTestServer({
  'https://api.example.com/endpoint': {
    response: { type: 'json-value', body: {...} },
  },
});
```
- Automatically handles `beforeAll()` server start, `beforeEach()` reset, `afterAll()` stop
- Provides `server.calls` array to inspect requests (URL, method, body, headers)
- Supports dynamic responses via functions: `(options: { callNumber: number }) => UrlResponse`

**Common Test Patterns:**

a) **Model instantiation & metadata:**
```typescript
const model = new OpenAIImageModel('dall-e-3', {
  provider: 'openai',
  url: () => 'https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations',
  headers: () => ({ /* ... */ }),
  _internal: { currentDate: () => testDate },
});
```

b) **doGenerate() invocation:**
```typescript
const result = await model.doGenerate({
  prompt: 'A cute baby sea otter',
  files: undefined,          // for image editing, pass file objects
  mask: undefined,           // for mask-based editing
  n: 1,                      // number of images
  size: '1024x1024',        // model-specific
  aspectRatio: undefined,    // if supported
  seed: undefined,           // if supported
  providerOptions: { openai: { style: 'vivid' } },
  headers: { /* request headers */ },
  abortSignal: controller.signal,
});
```

c) **Response validation:**
```typescript
expect(result.images).toStrictEqual(['base64-encoded-image']);
expect(result.usage).toStrictEqual({ inputTokens: 12, outputTokens: 0, totalTokens: 12 });
expect(result.response).toMatchObject({
  timestamp: Date,
  modelId: 'dalle-3',
  headers: { 'x-request-id': '...', 'content-type': '...' },
});
expect(result.warnings).toMatchInlineSnapshot([...]);
expect(result.providerMetadata?.openai?.images[0]).toMatchObject({
  seed: 12345,
  created: 1733837122,
  revisedPrompt: 'enhanced prompt',
});
```

d) **Request inspection:**
```typescript
const requestBody = await server.calls[0].requestBodyJson;
expect(requestBody).toStrictEqual({ model: 'dall-e-3', prompt: '...', ... });
expect(server.calls[0].requestHeaders).toStrictEqual({ authorization: 'Bearer ...', ... });
expect(server.calls[0].requestMethod).toBe('POST');
```

**Fixture Patterns:**
- Minimal fixtures with only used properties
- Use `.nullish()` in Zod schemas (not `.optional()`) for API flexibility
- Load and transform fixtures per test with helper functions:
```typescript
function prepareJsonFixtureResponse(filename: string, headers?: Record<string, string>) {
  server.urls['https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations'].response = {
    type: 'json-value',
    headers,
    body: JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(`src/image/__fixtures__/${filename}.json`, 'utf8')),
  };
}
```

**Key Test Cases Found (from `packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.test.ts`):**
- Parameters passed correctly (model, settings, provider options)
- Headers merged (provider + request-level headers)
- Images extracted from response
- Warnings for unsupported settings
- Model-specific constraints (e.g., `maxImagesPerCall`)
- Response metadata (timestamp, modelId, headers)
- Usage token mapping
- Image editing flows (form data, multipart, file handling)
- Error handling with proper status codes

---

### 2. BUILD CONFIGURATION & TSCONFIG

**Root tsconfig.json Structure:**
- Located at `/tsconfig.json`
- Uses **TypeScript Project References** for monorepo management
- References all packages and tools:
```json
{
  "references": [
    { "path": "packages/ai" },
    { "path": "packages/openai" },
    { "path": "packages/provider-utils" },
    // ... 50+ packages
  ]
}
```

**Package-level tsconfig.json Pattern (e.g., `packages/openai/tsconfig.json`):**
```json
{
  "extends": "./node_modules/@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": true,
    "rootDir": "src",
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "exclude": ["dist", "build", "node_modules", "tsup.config.ts"],
  "references": [
    { "path": "../provider" },
    { "path": "../provider-utils" }
  ]
}
```

**Key Settings:**
- `extends`: Shared tsconfig from `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- `composite: true`: Enables incremental builds
- `rootDir: "src"`, `outDir: "dist"`: Standard source/output structure
- Package references: Dependencies declared explicitly

**Build Script Pattern:**
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm clean && tsup --tsconfig tsconfig.build.json",
    "type-check": "tsc --build",
    "test": "pnpm test:node && pnpm test:edge",
    "test:node": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js --run",
    "test:edge": "vitest --config vitest.edge.config.js --run"
  }
}
```

**Test Configuration (`vitest.node.config.js`):**
```javascript
export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    environment: 'node',
    include: ['**/*.test.ts', '**/*.test.tsx'],
  },
  define: {
    __PACKAGE_VERSION__: JSON.stringify(version),
  },
});
```

---

### 3. PNPM WORKSPACE & PACKAGE REGISTRATION

**pnpm-workspace.yaml:**
```yaml
packages:
  - 'packages/*'      # All provider packages
  - 'tools/*'         # Tooling packages
  - 'examples/*'      # Example applications
  - 'packages/rsc/tests/e2e/next-server'  # Special case
```

**Workspace Dependencies Pattern (in package.json):**
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/provider": "workspace:*",
    "@ai-sdk/provider-utils": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```
- Uses `workspace:*` to link local packages
- Dependencies automatically resolve within monorepo
- No version numbers needed for local packages

**Standard Package Scripts (from `packages/openai/package.json`):**
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm clean && tsup --tsconfig tsconfig.build.json",
    "build:watch": "pnpm clean && tsup --watch",
    "clean": "del-cli dist docs *.tsbuildinfo",
    "prepack": "mkdir -p docs && cp ../../content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/03-openai.mdx ./docs/",
    "postpack": "del-cli docs",
    "type-check": "tsc --build",
    "test": "pnpm test:node && pnpm test:edge",
    "test:update": "pnpm test:node -u",
    "test:watch": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js",
    "test:edge": "vitest --config vitest.edge.config.js --run",
    "test:node": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js --run"
  }
}
```

**Exports Pattern:**
```json
{
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.js"
    },
    "./internal": {
      "types": "./dist/internal/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/internal/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/internal/index.js"
    }
  }
}
```

**Files Included in npm Package:**
```json
{
  "files": [
    "dist/**/*",
    "docs/**/*",
    "src",
    "!src/**/*.test.ts",
    "!src/**/*.test-d.ts",
    "!src/**/__snapshots__",
    "!src/**/__fixtures__",
    "CHANGELOG.md",
    "README.md"
  ]
}
```

---

### 4. PROVIDER-UTILS SHARED UTILITIES

**Location:** `/packages/provider-utils/src/`

**HTTP & API Handling Utilities:**
- `getFromApi.ts`: GET requests with response/error handlers
- `postJsonToApi.ts`: POST JSON with automatic JSON stringification
- `postFormDataToApi.ts`: POST FormData (for image uploads, multipart)
- `postToApi.ts`: Core POST implementation

**API Key & Configuration:**
- `loadApiKey.ts`: Validates/loads API key from params or env vars, throws `LoadAPIKeyError`
- `loadSetting.ts`: Required settings loader
- `loadOptionalSetting.ts`: Optional settings with defaults
- `withUserAgentSuffix.ts`: Adds SDK version to User-Agent headers

**Response Handling:**
- `response-handler.ts`: Factory functions for different response types:
  - `createJsonResponseHandler(schema)`: Parse JSON with validation
  - `createJsonErrorResponseHandler(errorSchema, errorToMessage)`: Error parsing
  - `createEventSourceResponseHandler(chunkSchema)`: SSE streaming
  - `createBinaryResponseHandler()`: Binary blob handling
  - `createStatusCodeErrorResponseHandler()`: Generic status code errors

**Error Handling:**
- `handleFetchError.ts`: Maps network errors to `APICallError` with retry logic
- `is-abort-error.ts`: Detects abort/cancellation errors
- Error details: statusCode, responseBody, requestBodyValues, cause, isRetryable flag

**Data Format Conversions:**
- `convertToFormData.ts`: Converts objects to FormData for multipart requests
- `convertImageModelFileToDataUri.ts`: Base64/file to data URIs
- `convertBase64ToUint8Array.ts`: Base64 string to binary
- `downloadBlob.ts`: Fetch remote file as Uint8Array with size limiting

**Parsing & Validation:**
- `parseJson.ts`: Safe JSON parse with schema validation (returns `ParseResult<T>`)
- `parseJsonEventStream.ts`: Parse Server-Sent Events stream
- `schema.ts`: Flexible schema support (Zod, Standard Schema, lazy evaluation)
- `secure-json-parse.ts`: Safe JSON parsing without prototype pollution

**Utilities:**
- `combineHeaders.ts`: Merge provider + request headers
- `normalizeHeaders.ts`: Normalize header names to lowercase
- `extractResponseHeaders.ts`: Extract headers from Response object
- `delay.ts`, `delayed-promise.ts`: Timing utilities
- `generateId.ts`: ID generation for requests
- `media-type-to-extension.ts`: MIME type to file extension mapping

---

### 5. ERROR HANDLING PATTERNS

**Core Pattern:**
1. Catch fetch errors using `handleFetchError()` which wraps in `APICallError`
2. Use typed response handlers that parse AND handle errors:
```typescript
const { value: response, responseHeaders } = await postJsonToApi({
  url: '...',
  headers: { /* ... */ },
  body: { /* ... */ },
  failedResponseHandler: createJsonErrorResponseHandler({
    errorSchema: zodSchema(z.object({ error: z.string() })),
    errorToMessage: (error) => error.error,
    isRetryable: (response) => response.status >= 500,
  }),
  successfulResponseHandler: createJsonResponseHandler(responseSchema),
});
```

**Error Mapping Example (from `packages/openai/src/openai-error.ts`):**
- Create provider-specific error handlers
- Map API error formats to `APICallError`
- Include retryable logic based on status codes

**NetworkError Handling (`handleFetchError.ts`):**
```typescript
- Detects TypeError with "fetch failed" message
- Unwraps underlying network cause
- Returns `APICallError` with `isRetryable: true`
- Handles Bun-specific error codes
```

**Response Handler Contract:**
```typescript
type ResponseHandler<T> = (options: {
  url: string;
  requestBodyValues: unknown;
  response: Response;
}) => PromiseLike<{
  value: T;
  rawValue?: unknown;
  responseHeaders?: Record<string, string>;
}>;
```

---

### 6. CONTRIBUTING GUIDES FOUND

**Key Documents:**
1. **`contributing/add-new-provider.md`** - High-level provider creation
   - Create folder `packages/<provider>`
   - Set version to `0.0.0`
   - Create changeset with `major`
   - Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts`
   - Add documentation to `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/`

2. **`contributing/provider-architecture.md`** - Architecture overview
   - Layered adapter pattern
   - Dependencies: `ai` → `@ai-sdk/provider` & `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` → individual providers

3. **`contributing/providers.md`** - Provider development notes
   - Provider options: use `.optional()` (restrictive, more future flexibility)
   - Response schemas: use `.nullish()` (flexible for API changes)
   - Type naming: `{Provider}{ModelType}Options` (PascalCase for types, camelCase for Zod)
   - Method names: fully specified with "Model" suffix (`imageModel()`, `languageModel()`)

4. **`contributing/testing.md`** - Testing guidelines
   - Manual testing via `/examples/ai-functions` and `/examples/ai-e2e-next`
   - Coverage: `generateText` (CLI), `streamText` (CLI), UI test (web)
   - Fixtures: store true provider responses in `__fixtures__` folders
   - SSE formats vary by provider (OpenAI-style vs Event-typed)
   - Fixture loaders must reconstruct SSE format

5. **`contributing/add-new-model.md`** - Adding new model support
   - Add model ID to settings
   - Update provider documentation page
   - PR with changeset
   - NPM publish via Changesets

---

### 7. PROVIDER PACKAGE STRUCTURE

**Standard Directory Layout (e.g., `packages/openai/`):**
```
src/
  ├── index.ts                          # Main exports
  ├── openai-provider.ts                # Provider factory & interface
  ├── openai-provider.test.ts           # Provider tests
  ├── version.ts                        # Package version constant
  ├── image/
  │   ├── openai-image-model.ts        # ImageModelV3 implementation
  │   ├── openai-image-model.test.ts   # Image model tests
  │   ├── openai-image-api.ts          # Response schema (Zod)
  │   ├── openai-image-options.ts      # Model IDs and config
  │   └── __fixtures__/
  │       ├── openai-image.json        # Generation response
  │       └── openai-image-edit.json   # Editing response
  ├── chat/                             # Chat language models
  ├── embedding/                        # Embedding models
  ├── transcription/                    # Speech-to-text
  └── speech/                           # Text-to-speech
package.json                            # Package metadata
tsconfig.json                          # TypeScript config
vitest.node.config.js                  # Node test config
vitest.edge.config.js                  # Edge runtime test config
```

**Provider Implementation Pattern:**
```typescript
// 1. Define provider interface
export interface LumaProvider extends ProviderV3 {
  image(modelId: LumaImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
  imageModel(modelId: LumaImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
  // ... other model types
}

// 2. Create provider factory function
export function createLuma(options: LumaProviderSettings = {}): LumaProvider {
  const baseURL = withoutTrailingSlash(options.baseURL ?? defaultBaseURL);
  const getHeaders = () => ({
    Authorization: `Bearer ${loadApiKey({...})}`,
    ...options.headers,
  });

  const createImageModel = (modelId: LumaImageModelId) =>
    new LumaImageModel(modelId, {
      provider: 'luma.image',
      baseURL,
      headers: getHeaders,
      fetch: options.fetch,
    });

  return {
    specificationVersion: 'v3',
    image: createImageModel,
    imageModel: createImageModel,
    // ... other model constructors
  };
}

// 3. Export singleton instance
export const luma = createLuma();

// 4. Export types from index.ts
export { createLuma, luma };
export type { LumaProvider, LumaProviderSettings };
```

**ImageModelV3 Implementation Pattern:**
```typescript
export class OpenAIImageModel implements ImageModelV3 {
  readonly specificationVersion = 'v3';
  
  get maxImagesPerCall(): number {
    return modelMaxImagesPerCall[this.modelId] ?? 1;
  }

  constructor(
    readonly modelId: OpenAIImageModelId,
    private readonly config: OpenAIImageModelConfig,
  ) {}

  async doGenerate({
    prompt, files, mask, n, size, aspectRatio, seed,
    providerOptions, headers, abortSignal,
  }: Parameters<ImageModelV3['doGenerate']>[0]) {
    // Implementation
    return {
      images: ['base64-string1', 'base64-string2'],
      warnings: [...],
      usage: { inputTokens, outputTokens, totalTokens },
      response: { timestamp, modelId, headers },
      providerMetadata: { openai: { images: [...] } },
    };
  }
}
```

---

### KEY FILES & PATHS

**Configuration:**
- `/tsconfig.json` - Root project references
- `/pnpm-workspace.yaml` - Workspace package configuration
- `/packages/provider-utils/` - Shared utilities location

**Documentation:**
- `/contributing/add-new-provider.md` - Provider creation guide
- `/contributing/provider-architecture.md` - Architecture overview
- `/contributing/providers.md` - Development notes
- `/contributing/testing.md` - Testing guidelines

**Test Patterns:**
- `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.test.ts` - Complete example
- `/packages/google/src/google-generative-ai-image-model.test.ts` - Multi-model example
- `/packages/black-forest-labs/src/black-forest-labs-image-model.test.ts` - Advanced polling
- `/packages/luma/src/luma-image-model.test.ts` - Simple provider

**Provider Examples:**
- `/packages/openai/` - Full-featured multi-model provider
- `/packages/luma/` - Image-only provider
- `/packages/google/` - Complex multi-variant provider

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Summary: Testing Patterns, Build Configuration, and Package Setup in Vercel AI SDK Monorepo

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. Here are the comprehensive findings:

---

### 1. IMAGE MODEL TESTING PATTERNS

**Test Structure & Location:**
- Test files are co-located with source files: `*-image-model.test.ts` (e.g., `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.test.ts`)
- Tests use **Vitest** with a test server powered by `@ai-sdk/test-server`
- Test fixtures stored in `__fixtures__` subdirectories (e.g., `/packages/openai/src/image/__fixtures__/`)

**Test Server Setup (from `packages/test-server/src/with-vitest.ts`):**
```typescript
const server = createTestServer({
  'https://api.example.com/endpoint': {
    response: { type: 'json-value', body: {...} },
  },
});
```
- Automatically handles `beforeAll()` server start, `beforeEach()` reset, `afterAll()` stop
- Provides `server.calls` array to inspect requests (URL, method, body, headers)
- Supports dynamic responses via functions: `(options: { callNumber: number }) => UrlResponse`

**Common Test Patterns:**

a) **Model instantiation & metadata:**
```typescript
const model = new OpenAIImageModel('dall-e-3', {
  provider: 'openai',
  url: () => 'https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations',
  headers: () => ({ /* ... */ }),
  _internal: { currentDate: () => testDate },
});
```

b) **doGenerate() invocation:**
```typescript
const result = await model.doGenerate({
  prompt: 'A cute baby sea otter',
  files: undefined,          // for image editing, pass file objects
  mask: undefined,           // for mask-based editing
  n: 1,                      // number of images
  size: '1024x1024',        // model-specific
  aspectRatio: undefined,    // if supported
  seed: undefined,           // if supported
  providerOptions: { openai: { style: 'vivid' } },
  headers: { /* request headers */ },
  abortSignal: controller.signal,
});
```

c) **Response validation:**
```typescript
expect(result.images).toStrictEqual(['base64-encoded-image']);
expect(result.usage).toStrictEqual({ inputTokens: 12, outputTokens: 0, totalTokens: 12 });
expect(result.response).toMatchObject({
  timestamp: Date,
  modelId: 'dalle-3',
  headers: { 'x-request-id': '...', 'content-type': '...' },
});
expect(result.warnings).toMatchInlineSnapshot([...]);
expect(result.providerMetadata?.openai?.images[0]).toMatchObject({
  seed: 12345,
  created: 1733837122,
  revisedPrompt: 'enhanced prompt',
});
```

d) **Request inspection:**
```typescript
const requestBody = await server.calls[0].requestBodyJson;
expect(requestBody).toStrictEqual({ model: 'dall-e-3', prompt: '...', ... });
expect(server.calls[0].requestHeaders).toStrictEqual({ authorization: 'Bearer ...', ... });
expect(server.calls[0].requestMethod).toBe('POST');
```

**Fixture Patterns:**
- Minimal fixtures with only used properties
- Use `.nullish()` in Zod schemas (not `.optional()`) for API flexibility
- Load and transform fixtures per test with helper functions:
```typescript
function prepareJsonFixtureResponse(filename: string, headers?: Record<string, string>) {
  server.urls['https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations'].response = {
    type: 'json-value',
    headers,
    body: JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(`src/image/__fixtures__/${filename}.json`, 'utf8')),
  };
}
```

**Key Test Cases Found (from `packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.test.ts`):**
- Parameters passed correctly (model, settings, provider options)
- Headers merged (provider + request-level headers)
- Images extracted from response
- Warnings for unsupported settings
- Model-specific constraints (e.g., `maxImagesPerCall`)
- Response metadata (timestamp, modelId, headers)
- Usage token mapping
- Image editing flows (form data, multipart, file handling)
- Error handling with proper status codes

---

### 2. BUILD CONFIGURATION & TSCONFIG

**Root tsconfig.json Structure:**
- Located at `/tsconfig.json`
- Uses **TypeScript Project References** for monorepo management
- References all packages and tools:
```json
{
  "references": [
    { "path": "packages/ai" },
    { "path": "packages/openai" },
    { "path": "packages/provider-utils" },
    // ... 50+ packages
  ]
}
```

**Package-level tsconfig.json Pattern (e.g., `packages/openai/tsconfig.json`):**
```json
{
  "extends": "./node_modules/@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": true,
    "rootDir": "src",
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "exclude": ["dist", "build", "node_modules", "tsup.config.ts"],
  "references": [
    { "path": "../provider" },
    { "path": "../provider-utils" }
  ]
}
```

**Key Settings:**
- `extends`: Shared tsconfig from `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- `composite: true`: Enables incremental builds
- `rootDir: "src"`, `outDir: "dist"`: Standard source/output structure
- Package references: Dependencies declared explicitly

**Build Script Pattern:**
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm clean && tsup --tsconfig tsconfig.build.json",
    "type-check": "tsc --build",
    "test": "pnpm test:node && pnpm test:edge",
    "test:node": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js --run",
    "test:edge": "vitest --config vitest.edge.config.js --run"
  }
}
```

**Test Configuration (`vitest.node.config.js`):**
```javascript
export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    environment: 'node',
    include: ['**/*.test.ts', '**/*.test.tsx'],
  },
  define: {
    __PACKAGE_VERSION__: JSON.stringify(version),
  },
});
```

---

### 3. PNPM WORKSPACE & PACKAGE REGISTRATION

**pnpm-workspace.yaml:**
```yaml
packages:
  - 'packages/*'      # All provider packages
  - 'tools/*'         # Tooling packages
  - 'examples/*'      # Example applications
  - 'packages/rsc/tests/e2e/next-server'  # Special case
```

**Workspace Dependencies Pattern (in package.json):**
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/provider": "workspace:*",
    "@ai-sdk/provider-utils": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```
- Uses `workspace:*` to link local packages
- Dependencies automatically resolve within monorepo
- No version numbers needed for local packages

**Standard Package Scripts (from `packages/openai/package.json`):**
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm clean && tsup --tsconfig tsconfig.build.json",
    "build:watch": "pnpm clean && tsup --watch",
    "clean": "del-cli dist docs *.tsbuildinfo",
    "prepack": "mkdir -p docs && cp ../../content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/03-openai.mdx ./docs/",
    "postpack": "del-cli docs",
    "type-check": "tsc --build",
    "test": "pnpm test:node && pnpm test:edge",
    "test:update": "pnpm test:node -u",
    "test:watch": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js",
    "test:edge": "vitest --config vitest.edge.config.js --run",
    "test:node": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js --run"
  }
}
```

**Exports Pattern:**
```json
{
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.js"
    },
    "./internal": {
      "types": "./dist/internal/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/internal/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/internal/index.js"
    }
  }
}
```

**Files Included in npm Package:**
```json
{
  "files": [
    "dist/**/*",
    "docs/**/*",
    "src",
    "!src/**/*.test.ts",
    "!src/**/*.test-d.ts",
    "!src/**/__snapshots__",
    "!src/**/__fixtures__",
    "CHANGELOG.md",
    "README.md"
  ]
}
```

---

### 4. PROVIDER-UTILS SHARED UTILITIES

**Location:** `/packages/provider-utils/src/`

**HTTP & API Handling Utilities:**
- `getFromApi.ts`: GET requests with response/error handlers
- `postJsonToApi.ts`: POST JSON with automatic JSON stringification

- `/packages/google/` - Complex multi-variant provider

file.content:
# Add new provider

## `@ai-sdk/<provider>` vs 3rd party package

Every provider is welcome to create a 3rd party package. We are happy to link to it from our documentation.

If you would prefer a 1st party `@ai-sdk/<provider>` package, please create an issue first to discuss.

## Example

https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8136/files

## How

1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
2. Set version in `packages/<provider>/package.json` to `0.0.0`
3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts` depending on what model types the provider supports
5. Add documentation in `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/<last number + 10>-<provider>.mdx`

See also [providers.md](providers.md)

## When in pre-release mode

If `main` is set up to publish `beta` releases, no further action is necessary. Just make sure not to backport it to the `vX.Y` stable branch since it will result in an npm version conflict once we exit pre-release mode on `main`

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a new fictional "PixelArt" AI provider package to the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. This provider ONLY supports image generation (no text, chat, embeddings). It should be simple and synchronous (no polling).

## Requirements
- Package name: `@ai-sdk/pixelart`
- Package directory: `packages/pixelart/`
- Export a `createPixelArt` factory function and a `pixelArt` default instance
- Support image settings for:
  - `size` (standard ImageModelV3 option)
  - `style` ("pixel", "retro", "8bit") — via provider options
  - `pixelDensity` (number) — via provider options
- Base URL: `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- API key env var: `PIXELART_API_KEY`
- Auth header: `Authorization: Bearer <key>`
- Do NOT modify any existing files/packages

## Codebase Context

### Reference Implementation: Black Forest Labs (image-only provider)
This is the closest template. Located at `packages/black-forest-labs/`.

Key files:
- `packages/black-forest-labs/package.json` — version 1.0.27, deps: `@ai-sdk/provider` (workspace:*), `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace:*), devDeps: `@ai-sdk/test-server`, `@vercel/ai-tsconfig`, `tsup`, `typescript`, `zod`. peerDeps: `zod`. Exports pattern: `.` with types/import/require, plus `./package.json`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.json` — extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`, composite: true, rootDir: src, outDir: dist, references to `../provider`, `../provider-utils`, `../test-server`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.build.json` — extends `./tsconfig.json`, sets `composite: false`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/tsup.config.ts` — entry: `src/index.ts`, format: cjs+esm, dts: true, sourcemap: true, defines `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` from package.json.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.node.config.js` — environment: node, includes `**/*.test.ts`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.edge.config.js` — environment: edge-runtime.

Source files:
- `src/index.ts` — exports `createBlackForestLabs`, `blackForestLabs`, types for provider/settings/model-id/options, and `VERSION`.
- `src/version.ts` — declares `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` and exports `VERSION` with fallback to '0.0.0-test'.
- `src/black-forest-labs-image-settings.ts` — exports `BlackForestLabsImageModelId` type union (string literals + `(string & {})`) and `BlackForestLabsAspectRatio` type.
- `src/black-forest-labs-provider.ts` — exports `BlackForestLabsProviderSettings` interface (apiKey, baseURL, headers, fetch, pollIntervalMillis, pollTimeoutMillis), `BlackForestLabsProvider` interface extending `ProviderV3` with `image()` and `imageModel()` methods + `textEmbeddingModel()` deprecated + throws `NoSuchModelError` for unsupported models. Factory function `createBlackForestLabs()` and default instance.
- `src/black-forest-labs-image-model.ts` — implements `ImageModelV3`. Has config interface, `doGenerate()` method, provider options schema using `lazySchema()`/`zodSchema()`/`z` from `zod/v4`, response schemas, error handler.

### ImageModelV3 Interface (from `@ai-sdk/provider`)
```typescript
type ImageModelV3 = {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3';
  readonly provider: string;
  readonly modelId: string;
  readonly maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | ((options) => ...);
  doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions): PromiseLike<{
    images: Array<string> | Array<Uint8Array>;
    warnings: Array<SharedV3Warning>;
    providerMetadata?: ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata;
    usage?: { inputTokens?, outputTokens?, totalTokens? };
    response: { timestamp: Date; modelId: string; headers: Record<string, string> | undefined };
  }>;
}
```

### Key Utilities from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`
- `loadApiKey({ apiKey, environmentVariableName, description })` — loads API key
- `withoutTrailingSlash(url)` — removes trailing slash
- `withUserAgentSuffix(headers, suffix)` — adds user agent
- `combineHeaders(...headers)` — merges headers
- `postJsonToApi({ url, headers, body, failedResponseHandler, successfulResponseHandler, abortSignal, fetch })` — POST JSON
- `createJsonResponseHandler(schema)` — JSON response handler
- `createJsonErrorResponseHandler({ errorSchema, errorToMessage })` — error handler
- `parseProviderOptions({ provider, providerOptions, schema })` — parse provider-specific options
- `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(...))` — lazy schema wrapper
- `zodSchema(z.object({...}))` — zod schema wrapper
- `resolve(resolvable)` — resolve Resolvable values

### Imports Pattern
- `import { z } from 'zod/v4';` (Zod 4)
- `import type { ImageModelV3, SharedV3Warning, NoSuchModelError, ProviderV3 } from '@ai-sdk/provider';`
- `import type { FetchFunction, Resolvable, InferSchema } from '@ai-sdk/provider-utils';`

### Contributing Guide says:
1. Create folder `packages/<provider>`
2. Set version to `0.0.0`
3. Create changeset with `major`
4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts`
5. Add documentation

### Root tsconfig.json
Has a `references` array — would need `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` added, but since we can't modify existing files, note this for documentation only.

## Design the Implementation

For the PixelArt provider, since it's fictional and simpler than BFL:
- No polling needed — the API returns images directly as base64 JSON (like OpenAI)
- Support multiple images per call (maxImagesPerCall = 4)
- Support 3 model IDs: `pixel-art-v1`, `pixel-art-hd`, `pixel-art-mini`
- The `doGenerate` method POSTs to `${baseURL}/images/generate` with JSON body
- Response format: `{ images: [{ b64_json: "..." }], created: number }`
- Provider options: `style` (enum: "pixel" | "retro" | "8bit"), `pixelDensity` (number 1-100)

Please provide a complete, file-by-file implementation plan with all the code structures needed. List every file that needs to be created.

CONTRIBUTING.md

1	# Contributing to the AI SDK
2	
3	We deeply appreciate your interest in contributing to our repository! Whether you're reporting bugs, suggesting enhancements, improving docs, or submitting pull requests, your contributions help improve the project for everyone.
4	
5	## Reporting Bugs
6	
7	If you've encountered a bug in the project, we encourage you to report it to us. Please follow these steps:
8	
9	1. **Check the Issue Tracker**: Before submitting a new bug report, please check our issue tracker to see if the bug has already been reported. If it has, you can add to the existing report.
10	2. **Create a New Issue**: If the bug hasn't been reported, create a new issue. Provide a clear title and a detailed description of the bug. Include any relevant logs, error messages, and steps to reproduce the issue.
11	3. **Label Your Issue**: If possible, label your issue as a `bug` so it's easier for maintainers to identify.
12	
13	## Suggesting Enhancements
14	
15	We're always looking for suggestions to make our project better. If you have an idea for an enhancement, please:
16	
17	1. **Check the Issue Tracker**: Similar to bug reports, please check if someone else has already suggested the enhancement. If so, feel free to add your thoughts to the existing issue.
18	2. **Create a New Issue**: If your enhancement hasn't been suggested yet, create a new issue. Provide a detailed description of your suggested enhancement and how it would benefit the project.
19	
20	## Improving Documentation
21	
22	Documentation is crucial for understanding and using our project effectively.
23	You can find the content of our docs under [`content`](https://github.com/vercel/ai/tree/main/content).
24	
25	To fix smaller typos, you can edit the code directly in GitHub or use Github.dev (press `.` in Github).
26	
27	If you want to make larger changes, please check out the Code Contributions section below. It also explains how to fix prettier issues that you might encounter during your docs changes.
28	
29	## Code Contributions
30	
31	We welcome your contributions to our code and documentation. Here's how you can contribute:
32	
33	### Environment Setup
34	
35	AI SDK development requires PNPM v9 (lockfile version) or higher and Node v22.
36	
37	### Setting Up the Repository Locally
38	
39	To set up the repository on your local machine, follow these steps:
40	
41	1. **Fork the Repository**: Make a copy of the repository to your GitHub account.
42	2. **Clone the Repository**: Clone the repository to your local machine, e.g. using `git clone`.
43	3. **Install Node**: If you haven't already, install Node v22.
44	4. **Install pnpm**: If you haven't already, install pnpm v10. You can do this by running `npm install -g pnpm@10` if you're using npm. Alternatively, if you're using Homebrew (Mac), you can run `brew install pnpm`. For more see [the pnpm site](https://pnpm.io/installation).
45	5. **Install Dependencies**: Navigate to the project directory and run `pnpm install` to install all necessary dependencies. This also sets up Git hooks via Husky.
46	6. **Build the Project**: Run `pnpm build` in the root to build all packages.
47	
48	### Running the Examples
49	
50	1. `cd examples/ai-functions` (for AI SDK Core, or another example folder)
51	1. AI SDK Core examples: run e.g. `pnpm tsx src/stream-text/openai.ts`
52	   - For most examples, you need to provide relevant API keys, e.g. environment variables like `OPENAI_API_KEY`
53	1. Other framework examples: run `pnpm dev` and go to the browser url
54	
55	### Local Development Workflow
56	
57	#### Building Packages
58	
59	To build the package that you're working on, run `pnpm build` or `pnpm build:watch` in the package folder.
60	This command updates the `dist` folder with the new version of the package.
61	Once built, the new code is picked up by the examples.
62	
63	#### Testing Packages
64	
65	To test the package that you're working on, run `pnpm test` in the package folder.
66	You do not need to rebuild your package to test it (only dependencies need to be built).
67	Some packages like `ai` also have more details tests and watch mode, see their `package.json` for more information.
68	
69	#### Adding package dependencies
70	
71	Please run `pnpm update-references` in workspace root to update the `references` section in the `tsconfig.json` file.
72	
73	### Submitting Pull Requests
74	
75	We greatly appreciate your pull requests. Here are the steps to submit them:
76	
77	1. **Create a New Branch**: Initiate your changes in a fresh branch. It's recommended to name the branch in a manner that signifies the changes you're implementing.
78	2. **Add a patch changeset**: If you're updating any packages and want to ensure they're released, add a **patch** changeset to your branch by running `pnpm changeset` in the workspace root.
79	
80	   - **Please do not use minor or major changesets**, we'll let you know when you need to use a different changeset type than patch.
81	   - You don't need to select any of the `examples/*` packages, as they are not released.
82	
83	3. **Add a codemod**: If the change introduces a deprecation or a breaking change, add a codemod if possible. See [how to contribute codemods](contributing/codemods.md)
84	4. **Commit Your Changes**: Ensure your commits are succinct and clear, detailing what modifications have been made and the reasons behind them. We don't require a specific commit message format, but please be descriptive.
85	5. **Pre-commit hooks**: A pre-commit hook automatically formats your staged files using `lint-staged` when you commit. If you stage any `package.json` changes, `pnpm install` runs automatically to keep the lockfile in sync. If you need to skip these hooks (e.g., for work-in-progress commits), set `ARTISANAL_MODE=1` before committing: `ARTISANAL_MODE=1 git commit -m "message"`.
86	6. **Push the Changes to Your GitHub Repository**: After committing your changes, push them to your GitHub repository.
87	7. **Open a Pull Request**: Propose your changes for review. Furnish a lucid title and description of your contributions. Make sure to link any relevant issues your PR resolves. We use the following PR title format:
88	
89	   - `fix(package-name): description` or
90	   - `feat(package-name): description` or
91	   - `chore(package-name): description` etc.
92	
93	8. **Respond to Feedback**: Stay receptive to and address any feedback or alteration requests from the project maintainers.
94	
95	Thank you for contributing to the AI SDK! Your efforts help us improve the project for everyone.
96	
97	## Learn More
98	
99	We have additional contributor documentation in the `contributing/` folder.
