1	# Add new provider
2	
3	## `@ai-sdk/<provider>` vs 3rd party package
4	
5	Every provider is welcome to create a 3rd party package. We are happy to link to it from our documentation.
6	
7	If you would prefer a 1st party `@ai-sdk/<provider>` package, please create an issue first to discuss.
8	
9	## Example
10	
11	https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8136/files
12	
13	## How
14	
15	1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
16	2. Set version in `packages/<provider>/package.json` to `0.0.0`
17	3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
18	4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts` depending on what model types the provider supports
19	5. Add documentation in `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/<last number + 10>-<provider>.mdx`
20	
21	See also [providers.md](providers.md)
22	
23	## When in pre-release mode
24	
25	If `main` is set up to publish `beta` releases, no further action is necessary. Just make sure not to backport it to the `vX.Y` stable branch since it will result in an npm version conflict once we exit pre-release mode on `main`
26

1	# Provider Development Notes
2	
3	## Provider Options Schemas
4	
5	Provider options schemas are user facing.
6	We want them to be as restrictive as possible, so that we have more flexibility with future changes and allow for meaningful `null` values.
7	
8	- use `.optional()` unless `null` is meaningful
9	
10	## Response Schemas
11	
12	Response schemas need to be flexible enough to deal with provider API changes that do not affect our processing
13	to prevent unnecessary breakages.
14	
15	- keep them minimal (no unused properties)
16	- use `.nullish()` instead of `.optional()`
17	
18	## Provider-Specific Model Options Types
19	
20	Types and Zod schemas for the provider specific model options follow the pattern `{Provider}{ModelType}Options`, e.g. `AnthropicLanguageModelOptions`.
21	If a provider has multiple implementations for the same model type, add a qualifier: `{Provider}{ModelType}{Qualifier}Options`, e.g. `OpenAILanguageModelChatOptions` and `OpenAILanguageModelResponsesOptions`.
22	If options apply provider-wide rather than to a specific model type, use `{Provider}ProviderOptions` instead, e.g. `GatewayProviderOptions`.
23	
24	- types are PascalCase, Zod schemas are camelCase (e.g. `openaiLanguageModelChatOptions`)
25	- types must be exported from the provider package, Zod schemas must not
26	
27	## Provider Method Names
28	
29	For the Provider v3 interface, we require fully specified names with a "Model" suffix, e.g. `languageModel(id)` or `imageModel(id)`. These help with clarity for both developers and agents.

92	    }
93	
94	    const splitSize = size?.split('x');
95	    // https://docs.together.ai/reference/post_images-generations
96	    const { value: response, responseHeaders } = await postJsonToApi({
97	      url: `${this.config.baseURL}/images/generations`,
98	      headers: combineHeaders(this.config.headers(), headers),
99	      body: {
100	        model: this.modelId,
101	        prompt,
102	        seed,
103	        ...(n > 1 ? { n } : {}),
104	        ...(splitSize && {
105	          width: parseInt(splitSize[0]),
106	          height: parseInt(splitSize[1]),
107	        }),
108	        ...(imageUrl != null ? { image_url: imageUrl } : {}),
109	        response_format: 'base64',
110	        ...(togetheraiOptions ?? {}),
111	      },
112	      failedResponseHandler: createJsonErrorResponseHandler({
113	        errorSchema: togetheraiErrorSchema,
114	        errorToMessage: data => data.error.message,
115	      }),
116	      successfulResponseHandler: createJsonResponseHandler(
117	        togetheraiImageResponseSchema,
118	      ),
119	      abortSignal,
120	      fetch: this.config.fetch,
121	    });
122	
123	    return {
124	      images: response.data.map(item => item.b64_json),
125	      warnings,
126	      response: {
127	        timestamp: currentDate,
128	        modelId: this.modelId,
129	        headers: responseHeaders,
130	      },
131	    };
132	  }
133	}
134	
135	// limited version of the schema, focussed on what is needed for the implementation
136	// this approach limits breakages when the API changes and increases efficiency
137	const togetheraiImageResponseSchema = z.object({
138	  data: z.array(
139	    z.object({
140	      b64_json: z.string(),
141	    }),
142	  ),
143	});
144	
145	// limited version of the schema, focussed on what is needed for the implementation
146	// this approach limits breakages when the API changes and increases efficiency
147	const togetheraiErrorSchema = z.object({
148	  error: z.object({
149	    message: z.string(),
150	  }),
151	});
152	
153	/**
154	 * Provider options schema for Together AI image generation.
155	 */
156	export const togetheraiImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
157	  zodSchema(
158	    z
159	      .object({
160	        /**
161	         * Number of generation steps. Higher values can improve quality.
162	         */
163	        steps: z.number().nullish(),
164	
165	        /**
166	         * Guidance scale for image generation.
167	         */
168	        guidance: z.number().nullish(),
169	
170	        /**
171	         * Negative prompt to guide what to avoid.
172	         */
173	        negative_prompt: z.string().nullish(),
174	
175	        /**
176	         * Disable the safety checker for image generation.
177	         * When true, the API will not reject images flagged as potentially NSFW.
178	         * Not available for Flux Schnell Free and Flux Pro models.
179	         */
180	        disable_safety_checker: z.boolean().nullish(),
181	      })
182	      .passthrough(),
183	  ),
184	);
185	
186	export type TogetherAIImageModelOptions = InferSchema<
187	  typeof togetheraiImageModelOptionsSchema
188	>;

content.text:
The workspace uses `packages/*` glob, so a new `packages/pixelart/` directory will be automatically discovered. Now I have everything I need to write the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Provider Package

### Overview

This plan details the creation of a new `@ai-sdk/pixelart` package within the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. The package implements an image-only AI provider that supports synchronous (non-polling) image generation, returning base64 JSON directly. It follows the patterns established by the xai and togetherai image providers (direct b64_json response) rather than the black-forest-labs provider (which uses polling).

### Architecture Decisions

1. **Pattern choice**: Use the xai/togetherai direct-response pattern (no polling), not the BFL polling pattern. This is simpler and matches the requirement for synchronous operation.

2. **Provider options schema**: Use the `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(z.object({...})))` pattern from BFL/togetherai rather than xai's raw zod schema pattern. This is the more modern approach used by newer providers and produces the correct `InferSchema` type export.

3. **Error handling**: Create a dedicated error file following the xai pattern with a `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`.

4. **Auth**: Use `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header (like most providers) rather than BFL's custom `x-key` header.

5. **Multiple images**: Support `maxImagesPerCall = 4` and pass `n` to the API body, following xai's pattern of returning multiple b64_json entries in `response.data[]`.

6. **Provider options key**: `pixelArt` (camelCase, following the BFL pattern of `blackForestLabs`).

---

### File-by-File Implementation Plan

#### File 1: `packages/pixelart/package.json`

Create a standard package.json following the BFL template exactly, with these changes:
- `"name": "@ai-sdk/pixelart"`
- `"version": "0.0.0"` (as per contributing guide for new packages)
- Same `dependencies`: `@ai-sdk/provider` (workspace:*), `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace:*)
- Same `devDependencies`: `@ai-sdk/test-server` (workspace:*), `@vercel/ai-tsconfig` (workspace:*), `tsup` (^8), `typescript` (5.8.3), `zod` (3.25.76)
- Same `peerDependencies`: `zod` (^3.25.76 || ^4.1.8)
- Same `exports`, `files`, `scripts`, `engines`, `publishConfig` patterns
- Remove the `prepack`/`postpack` scripts referencing documentation MDX (or point them at a pixelart MDX path)
- `"license": "Apache-2.0"`, `"sideEffects": false`
- Same `main`, `module`, `types` pointing to dist/

#### File 2: `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "./node_modules/@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": true,
    "rootDir": "src",
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "exclude": ["dist", "build", "node_modules", "tsup.config.ts"],
  "references": [
    { "path": "../provider" },
    { "path": "../provider-utils" },
    { "path": "../test-server" }
  ]
}
```

#### File 3: `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "./tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": false
  }
}
```

#### File 4: `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts`

Identical to BFL's tsup.config.ts:
- Entry: `src/index.ts`
- Format: cjs + esm
- dts: true, sourcemap: true
- Defines `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` from package.json version

#### File 5: `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js`

Standard vitest config with `environment: 'node'`, include `**/*.test.ts` and `**/*.test.tsx`, define `__PACKAGE_VERSION__`.

#### File 6: `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js`

Same as above but with `environment: 'edge-runtime'`.

---

#### File 7: `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts`

```typescript
declare const __PACKAGE_VERSION__: string | undefined;
export const VERSION: string =
  typeof __PACKAGE_VERSION__ !== 'undefined'
    ? __PACKAGE_VERSION__
    : '0.0.0-test';
```

#### File 8: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`

Defines the model ID type union with the three supported model IDs:

```typescript
export type PixelArtImageModelId =
  | 'pixel-art-v1'
  | 'pixel-art-hd'
  | 'pixel-art-mini'
  | (string & {});
```

The `(string & {})` allows arbitrary strings while providing autocomplete for known IDs.

#### File 9: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-error.ts`

Defines the error schema and failed response handler following the xai pattern:

```typescript
import { createJsonErrorResponseHandler } from '@ai-sdk/provider-utils';
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

export const pixelArtErrorDataSchema = z.object({
  error: z.object({
    message: z.string(),
    type: z.string().nullish(),
    code: z.union([z.string(), z.number()]).nullish(),
  }),
});

export type PixelArtErrorData = z.infer<typeof pixelArtErrorDataSchema>;

export const pixelArtFailedResponseHandler = createJsonErrorResponseHandler({
  errorSchema: pixelArtErrorDataSchema,
  errorToMessage: data => data.error.message,
});
```

#### File 10: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts`

This is the core implementation file. Key structure:

**Config interface:**
```typescript
interface PixelArtImageModelConfig {
  provider: string;
  baseURL: string;
  headers: () => Record<string, string | undefined>;
  fetch?: FetchFunction;
  _internal?: {
    currentDate?: () => Date;
  };
}
```

No polling config needed (unlike BFL).

**Class: `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3`:**
- `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- `maxImagesPerCall = 4`
- `provider` getter returns `this.config.provider`
- Constructor takes `modelId: PixelArtImageModelId` and `config: PixelArtImageModelConfig`

**`doGenerate` method:**
- Destructure all parameters from `Parameters<ImageModelV3['doGenerate']>[0]`
- Build warnings array:
  - Warn if `aspectRatio` is provided (unsupported)
  - Warn if `seed` is provided (unsupported)
  - Warn if `mask` is provided (unsupported)
  - Warn if `files` is provided (unsupported -- this is a generation-only provider)
- Parse provider options using `parseProviderOptions({ provider: 'pixelArt', providerOptions, schema: pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema })`
- Build request body:
  ```typescript
  {
    model: this.modelId,
    prompt,
    n,
    size,  // pass size directly (standard ImageModelV3 option)
    ...(pixelArtOptions?.style != null ? { style: pixelArtOptions.style } : {}),
    ...(pixelArtOptions?.pixelDensity != null ? { pixel_density: pixelArtOptions.pixelDensity } : {}),
  }
  ```
- POST to `${this.config.baseURL}/images/generate` using `postJsonToApi`
- Use `createJsonResponseHandler(pixelArtImageResponseSchema)` for success
- Use `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` for errors
- Map response `data[].b64_json` to the images array
- Return the standard response shape with `images`, `warnings`, `response` (timestamp, modelId, headers), and `providerMetadata`

**Response schema (private):**
```typescript
const pixelArtImageResponseSchema = z.object({
  images: z.array(
    z.object({
      b64_json: z.string(),
    }),
  ),
  created: z.number().nullish(),
});
```

**Provider options schema (exported for type, not for value):**
```typescript
export const pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(
    z.object({
      style: z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional(),
      pixelDensity: z.number().min(1).max(100).optional(),
    }),
  ),
);

export type PixelArtImageModelOptions = InferSchema<
  typeof pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema
>;
```

Per the contributing guide: use `.optional()` for provider options (not `.nullish()`), and use `.nullish()` for response schemas.

#### File 11: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts`

**Settings interface:**
```typescript
export interface PixelArtProviderSettings {
  apiKey?: string;
  baseURL?: string;
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
  fetch?: FetchFunction;
}
```

No polling settings needed (unlike BFL).

**Provider interface:**
```typescript
export interface PixelArtProvider extends ProviderV3 {
  image(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
  imageModel(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
}
```

**Factory function `createPixelArt(options?)`:**
- Default base URL: `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- Use `withoutTrailingSlash` on the base URL
- Build headers using `loadApiKey({ apiKey: options.apiKey, environmentVariableName: 'PIXELART_API_KEY', description: 'PixelArt' })`
- Auth header: `Authorization: Bearer ${apiKey}` (standard Bearer pattern)
- Add user agent suffix using `withUserAgentSuffix(headers, 'ai-sdk/pixelart/${VERSION}')`
- Create `createImageModel` function that instantiates `PixelArtImageModel`
- Throw `NoSuchModelError` for `languageModel`, `embeddingModel`, and `textEmbeddingModel`
- Return the provider object with `specificationVersion: 'v3'`

**Default instance:**
```typescript
export const pixelArt = createPixelArt();
```

#### File 12: `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts`

Public API barrel file following the naming conventions:

```typescript
export { createPixelArt, pixelArt } from './pixelart-provider';
export type {
  PixelArtProvider,
  PixelArtProviderSettings,
} from './pixelart-provider';
export type { PixelArtImageModelId } from './pixelart-image-settings';
export type {
  PixelArtImageModelOptions,
  /** @deprecated Use `PixelArtImageModelOptions` instead. */
  PixelArtImageModelOptions as PixelArtImageProviderOptions,
} from './pixelart-image-model';
export type { PixelArtErrorData } from './pixelart-error';
export { VERSION } from './version';
```

Note: Following the existing convention, the `PixelArtImageProviderOptions` deprecated alias is included for consistency with other providers (xai, BFL).

---

### Test Files

#### File 13: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts`

Test the provider factory, following BFL's provider test pattern:

1. **Creates image models via `.image` and `.imageModel`** -- verify `provider`, `modelId`, `specificationVersion` properties.
2. **Configures baseURL and headers correctly** -- use `createTestServer`, verify the POST URL is `${baseURL}/images/generate`, verify `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header, verify custom headers are passed through, verify user agent includes `ai-sdk/pixelart/`.
3. **Throws NoSuchModelError for unsupported model types** -- test `languageModel()`, `embeddingModel()`.

#### File 14: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts`

Test the image model, following xai's image model test pattern:

1. **Constructor** -- verify `provider`, `modelId`, `specificationVersion`, `maxImagesPerCall`.
2. **doGenerate sends correct parameters** -- prompt, n, size, model in body; POST to correct URL.
3. **Passes provider options** -- style and pixelDensity via `providerOptions: { pixelArt: { style: 'retro', pixelDensity: 50 } }`.
4. **Returns base64 images** -- verify response mapping from `{ images: [{ b64_json: '...' }] }`.
5. **Merges provider and request headers** -- verify `combineHeaders` behavior.
6. **Warns for unsupported features** -- aspectRatio, seed, mask, files.
7. **Handles API errors** -- 400 status with error body.
8. **Response metadata** -- verify timestamp, modelId, headers in response.
9. **Respects abort signal** -- abort controller test.

---

### Additional Files

#### File 15: `.changeset/add-pixelart-provider.md`

A changeset file for the new package (major, as per contributing guide for new packages):

```markdown
---
'@ai-sdk/pixelart': major
---

Add PixelArt image generation provider.
```

---

### Files That Would Need Modification (documented, not implemented)

Per the requirements, no existing files should be modified. However, for completeness, the following would eventually need updates:

1. **`tsconfig.json` (root)**: Add `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` to references array (alphabetically between `packages/perplexity` and `packages/provider`).
2. **Documentation**: Add `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/XX-pixelart.mdx`.
3. **Examples**: Add `examples/ai-functions/src/generate-image/pixelart.ts`.

These are noted but NOT part of this implementation plan since the requirement states "do NOT modify any existing files/packages."

---

### Complete File Manifest

All files to create (13 new files in `packages/pixelart/`):

| # | File Path | Purpose |
|---|-----------|---------|
| 1 | `packages/pixelart/package.json` | Package manifest |
| 2 | `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config |
| 3 | `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json` | Build-specific TS config |
| 4 | `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts` | Bundle config |
| 5 | `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js` | Node test config |
| 6 | `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js` | Edge test config |
| 7 | `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts` | Package version constant |
| 8 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts` | Model ID type union |
| 9 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-error.ts` | Error schema + handler |
| 10 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts` | Core ImageModelV3 implementation |
| 11 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts` | Provider factory + default instance |
| 12 | `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts` | Public API barrel exports |
| 13 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts` | Provider tests |
| 14 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts` | Image model tests |
| 15 | `.changeset/add-pixelart-provider.md` | Changeset for release |

---

### Dependency Graph

```
@ai-sdk/pixelart
  ├── @ai-sdk/provider (types: ImageModelV3, ProviderV3, NoSuchModelError, SharedV3Warning)
  ├── @ai-sdk/provider-utils (utils: loadApiKey, withoutTrailingSlash, withUserAgentSuffix,
  │    combineHeaders, postJsonToApi, createJsonResponseHandler,
  │    createJsonErrorResponseHandler, parseProviderOptions, lazySchema, zodSchema)
  ├── zod/v4 (z - for schemas)
  └── @ai-sdk/test-server (devDep, for tests)
```

### Key Design Rationale

1. **No polling**: The requirement specifies synchronous operation. The API returns images directly as base64 in the JSON response body, similar to xai and togetherai. This eliminates the entire polling infrastructure that BFL needs (pollIntervalMillis, pollTimeoutMillis, pollForImageUrl, delay, getFromApi for polling, bflPollSchema, bflSubmitSchema).

2. **`size` as standard option**: Unlike BFL which converts size to aspect ratio, and unlike xai which warns that size is unsupported, PixelArt passes `size` directly to the API body since the requirements specify it as a supported standard ImageModelV3 option.

3. **Provider options key `pixelArt`**: Following the camelCase convention (`blackForestLabs`, `togetherai`, `xai`).

4. **`lazySchema` + `zodSchema` wrapper**: Even though xai uses raw zod schemas, the BFL/togetherai pattern with `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(...))` is preferred for proper `InferSchema` type extraction and lazy evaluation. This is the newer/recommended pattern.

5. **`maxImagesPerCall = 4`**: Set as a static number per the requirements. Some providers use a function for dynamic limits, but a static number is simpler and sufficient here.

6. **Bearer auth**: Standard HTTP Bearer token auth unlike BFL's custom `x-key` header.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts
- /private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts
- /private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/pixelart/src/index.ts
- /private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/pixelart/package.json
- /private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507187064-vwbt9i/packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts

tool_use_result.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding a new fictional "PixelArt" AI provider package to the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. This provider ONLY supports image generation (no text, chat, embeddings). It should be simple and synchronous (no polling).

## Requirements
- Package name: `@ai-sdk/pixelart`
- Package directory: `packages/pixelart/`
- Export a `createPixelArt` factory function and a `pixelArt` default instance
- Support image settings for:
  - `size` (standard ImageModelV3 option)
  - `style` ("pixel", "retro", "8bit") — via provider options
  - `pixelDensity` (number) — via provider options
- Base URL: `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- API key env var: `PIXELART_API_KEY`
- Auth header: `Authorization: Bearer <key>`
- Do NOT modify any existing files/packages

## Codebase Context

### Reference Implementation: Black Forest Labs (image-only provider)
This is the closest template. Located at `packages/black-forest-labs/`.

Key files:
- `packages/black-forest-labs/package.json` — version 1.0.27, deps: `@ai-sdk/provider` (workspace:*), `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace:*), devDeps: `@ai-sdk/test-server`, `@vercel/ai-tsconfig`, `tsup`, `typescript`, `zod`. peerDeps: `zod`. Exports pattern: `.` with types/import/require, plus `./package.json`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.json` — extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`, composite: true, rootDir: src, outDir: dist, references to `../provider`, `../provider-utils`, `../test-server`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.build.json` — extends `./tsconfig.json`, sets `composite: false`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/tsup.config.ts` — entry: `src/index.ts`, format: cjs+esm, dts: true, sourcemap: true, defines `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` from package.json.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.node.config.js` — environment: node, includes `**/*.test.ts`.
- `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.edge.config.js` — environment: edge-runtime.

Source files:
- `src/index.ts` — exports `createBlackForestLabs`, `blackForestLabs`, types for provider/settings/model-id/options, and `VERSION`.
- `src/version.ts` — declares `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` and exports `VERSION` with fallback to '0.0.0-test'.
- `src/black-forest-labs-image-settings.ts` — exports `BlackForestLabsImageModelId` type union (string literals + `(string & {})`) and `BlackForestLabsAspectRatio` type.
- `src/black-forest-labs-provider.ts` — exports `BlackForestLabsProviderSettings` interface (apiKey, baseURL, headers, fetch, pollIntervalMillis, pollTimeoutMillis), `BlackForestLabsProvider` interface extending `ProviderV3` with `image()` and `imageModel()` methods + `textEmbeddingModel()` deprecated + throws `NoSuchModelError` for unsupported models. Factory function `createBlackForestLabs()` and default instance.
- `src/black-forest-labs-image-model.ts` — implements `ImageModelV3`. Has config interface, `doGenerate()` method, provider options schema using `lazySchema()`/`zodSchema()`/`z` from `zod/v4`, response schemas, error handler.

### ImageModelV3 Interface (from `@ai-sdk/provider`)
```typescript
type ImageModelV3 = {
  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3';
  readonly provider: string;
  readonly modelId: string;
  readonly maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | ((options) => ...);
  doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions): PromiseLike<{
    images: Array<string> | Array<Uint8Array>;
    warnings: Array<SharedV3Warning>;
    providerMetadata?: ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata;
    usage?: { inputTokens?, outputTokens?, totalTokens? };
    response: { timestamp: Date; modelId: string; headers: Record<string, string> | undefined };
  }>;
}
```

### Key Utilities from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`
- `loadApiKey({ apiKey, environmentVariableName, description })` — loads API key
- `withoutTrailingSlash(url)` — removes trailing slash
- `withUserAgentSuffix(headers, suffix)` — adds user agent
- `combineHeaders(...headers)` — merges headers
- `postJsonToApi({ url, headers, body, failedResponseHandler, successfulResponseHandler, abortSignal, fetch })` — POST JSON
- `createJsonResponseHandler(schema)` — JSON response handler
- `createJsonErrorResponseHandler({ errorSchema, errorToMessage })` — error handler
- `parseProviderOptions({ provider, providerOptions, schema })` — parse provider-specific options
- `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(...))` — lazy schema wrapper
- `zodSchema(z.object({...}))` — zod schema wrapper
- `resolve(resolvable)` — resolve Resolvable values

### Imports Pattern
- `import { z } from 'zod/v4';` (Zod 4)
- `import type { ImageModelV3, SharedV3Warning, NoSuchModelError, ProviderV3 } from '@ai-sdk/provider';`
- `import type { FetchFunction, Resolvable, InferSchema } from '@ai-sdk/provider-utils';`

### Contributing Guide says:
1. Create folder `packages/<provider>`
2. Set version to `0.0.0`
3. Create changeset with `major`
4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts`
5. Add documentation

### Root tsconfig.json
Has a `references` array — would need `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` added, but since we can't modify existing files, note this for documentation only.

## Design the Implementation

For the PixelArt provider, since it's fictional and simpler than BFL:
- No polling needed — the API returns images directly as base64 JSON (like OpenAI)
- Support multiple images per call (maxImagesPerCall = 4)
- Support 3 model IDs: `pixel-art-v1`, `pixel-art-hd`, `pixel-art-mini`
- The `doGenerate` method POSTs to `${baseURL}/images/generate` with JSON body
- Response format: `{ images: [{ b64_json: "..." }], created: number }`
- Provider options: `style` (enum: "pixel" | "retro" | "8bit"), `pixelDensity` (number 1-100)

The workspace uses `packages/*` glob, so a new `packages/pixelart/` directory will be automatically discovered. Now I have everything I need to write the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Provider Package

### Overview

This plan details the creation of a new `@ai-sdk/pixelart` package within the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. The package implements an image-only AI provider that supports synchronous (non-polling) image generation, returning base64 JSON directly. It follows the patterns established by the xai and togetherai image providers (direct b64_json response) rather than the black-forest-labs provider (which uses polling).

### Architecture Decisions

1. **Pattern choice**: Use the xai/togetherai direct-response pattern (no polling), not the BFL polling pattern. This is simpler and matches the requirement for synchronous operation.

2. **Provider options schema**: Use the `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(z.object({...})))` pattern from BFL/togetherai rather than xai's raw zod schema pattern. This is the more modern approach used by newer providers and produces the correct `InferSchema` type export.

3. **Error handling**: Create a dedicated error file following the xai pattern with a `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`.

4. **Auth**: Use `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header (like most providers) rather than BFL's custom `x-key` header.

5. **Multiple images**: Support `maxImagesPerCall = 4` and pass `n` to the API body, following xai's pattern of returning multiple b64_json entries in `response.data[]`.

6. **Provider options key**: `pixelArt` (camelCase, following the BFL pattern of `blackForestLabs`).

---

### File-by-File Implementation Plan

#### File 1: `packages/pixelart/package.json`

Create a standard package.json following the BFL template exactly, with these changes:
- `"name": "@ai-sdk/pixelart"`
- `"version": "0.0.0"` (as per contributing guide for new packages)
- Same `dependencies`: `@ai-sdk/provider` (workspace:*), `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace:*)
- Same `devDependencies`: `@ai-sdk/test-server` (workspace:*), `@vercel/ai-tsconfig` (workspace:*), `tsup` (^8), `typescript` (5.8.3), `zod` (3.25.76)
- Same `peerDependencies`: `zod` (^3.25.76 || ^4.1.8)
- Same `exports`, `files`, `scripts`, `engines`, `publishConfig` patterns
- Remove the `prepack`/`postpack` scripts referencing documentation MDX (or point them at a pixelart MDX path)
- `"license": "Apache-2.0"`, `"sideEffects": false`
- Same `main`, `module`, `types` pointing to dist/

#### File 2: `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "./node_modules/@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": true,
    "rootDir": "src",
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "exclude": ["dist", "build", "node_modules", "tsup.config.ts"],
  "references": [
    { "path": "../provider" },
    { "path": "../provider-utils" },
    { "path": "../test-server" }
  ]
}
```

#### File 3: `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json`

```json

  "extends": "./tsconfig.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": false
  }
}
```

#### File 4: `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts`

Identical to BFL's tsup.config.ts:
- Entry: `src/index.ts`
- Format: cjs + esm
- dts: true, sourcemap: true
- Defines `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` from package.json version

#### File 5: `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js`

Standard vitest config with `environment: 'node'`, include `**/*.test.ts` and `**/*.test.tsx`, define `__PACKAGE_VERSION__`.

#### File 6: `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js`

Same as above but with `environment: 'edge-runtime'`.

---

#### File 7: `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts`

```typescript
declare const __PACKAGE_VERSION__: string | undefined;
export const VERSION: string =
  typeof __PACKAGE_VERSION__ !== 'undefined'
    ? __PACKAGE_VERSION__
    : '0.0.0-test';
```

#### File 8: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`

Defines the model ID type union with the three supported model IDs:

```typescript
export type PixelArtImageModelId =
  | 'pixel-art-v1'
  | 'pixel-art-hd'
  | 'pixel-art-mini'
  | (string & {});
```

The `(string & {})` allows arbitrary strings while providing autocomplete for known IDs.

#### File 9: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-error.ts`

Defines the error schema and failed response handler following the xai pattern:

```typescript
import { createJsonErrorResponseHandler } from '@ai-sdk/provider-utils';
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

export const pixelArtErrorDataSchema = z.object({
  error: z.object({
    message: z.string(),
    type: z.string().nullish(),
    code: z.union([z.string(), z.number()]).nullish(),
  }),
});

export type PixelArtErrorData = z.infer<typeof pixelArtErrorDataSchema>;

export const pixelArtFailedResponseHandler = createJsonErrorResponseHandler({
  errorSchema: pixelArtErrorDataSchema,
  errorToMessage: data => data.error.message,
});
```

#### File 10: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts`

This is the core implementation file. Key structure:

**Config interface:**
```typescript
interface PixelArtImageModelConfig {
  provider: string;
  baseURL: string;
  headers: () => Record<string, string | undefined>;
  fetch?: FetchFunction;
  _internal?: {
    currentDate?: () => Date;
  };
}
```

No polling config needed (unlike BFL).

**Class: `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3`:**
- `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- `maxImagesPerCall = 4`
- `provider` getter returns `this.config.provider`
- Constructor takes `modelId: PixelArtImageModelId` and `config: PixelArtImageModelConfig`

**`doGenerate` method:**
- Destructure all parameters from `Parameters<ImageModelV3['doGenerate']>[0]`
- Build warnings array:
  - Warn if `aspectRatio` is provided (unsupported)
  - Warn if `seed` is provided (unsupported)
  - Warn if `mask` is provided (unsupported)
  - Warn if `files` is provided (unsupported -- this is a generation-only provider)
- Parse provider options using `parseProviderOptions({ provider: 'pixelArt', providerOptions, schema: pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema })`
- Build request body:
  ```typescript
  {
    model: this.modelId,
    prompt,
    n,
    size,  // pass size directly (standard ImageModelV3 option)
    ...(pixelArtOptions?.style != null ? { style: pixelArtOptions.style } : {}),
    ...(pixelArtOptions?.pixelDensity != null ? { pixel_density: pixelArtOptions.pixelDensity } : {}),
  }
  ```
- POST to `${this.config.baseURL}/images/generate` using `postJsonToApi`
- Use `createJsonResponseHandler(pixelArtImageResponseSchema)` for success
- Use `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` for errors
- Map response `data[].b64_json` to the images array
- Return the standard response shape with `images`, `warnings`, `response` (timestamp, modelId, headers), and `providerMetadata`

**Response schema (private):**
```typescript
const pixelArtImageResponseSchema = z.object({
  images: z.array(
    z.object({
      b64_json: z.string(),
    }),
  ),
  created: z.number().nullish(),
});
```

**Provider options schema (exported for type, not for value):**
```typescript
export const pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(
    z.object({
      style: z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional(),
      pixelDensity: z.number().min(1).max(100).optional(),
    }),
  ),
);

export type PixelArtImageModelOptions = InferSchema<
  typeof pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema
>;
```

Per the contributing guide: use `.optional()` for provider options (not `.nullish()`), and use `.nullish()` for response schemas.

#### File 11: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts`

**Settings interface:**
```typescript
export interface PixelArtProviderSettings {
  apiKey?: string;
  baseURL?: string;
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
  fetch?: FetchFunction;
}
```

No polling settings needed (unlike BFL).

**Provider interface:**
```typescript
export interface PixelArtProvider extends ProviderV3 {
  image(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
  imageModel(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
}
```

**Factory function `createPixelArt(options?)`:**
- Default base URL: `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- Use `withoutTrailingSlash` on the base URL
- Build headers using `loadApiKey({ apiKey: options.apiKey, environmentVariableName: 'PIXELART_API_KEY', description: 'PixelArt' })`
- Auth header: `Authorization: Bearer ${apiKey}` (standard Bearer pattern)
- Add user agent suffix using `withUserAgentSuffix(headers, 'ai-sdk/pixelart/${VERSION}')`
- Create `createImageModel` function that instantiates `PixelArtImageModel`
- Throw `NoSuchModelError` for `languageModel`, `embeddingModel`, and `textEmbeddingModel`
- Return the provider object with `specificationVersion: 'v3'`

**Default instance:**
```typescript
export const pixelArt = createPixelArt();
```

#### File 12: `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts`

Public API barrel file following the naming conventions:

```typescript
export { createPixelArt, pixelArt } from './pixelart-provider';
export type {
  PixelArtProvider,
  PixelArtProviderSettings,
} from './pixelart-provider';
export type { PixelArtImageModelId } from './pixelart-image-settings';
export type {
  PixelArtImageModelOptions,
  /** @deprecated Use `PixelArtImageModelOptions` instead. */
  PixelArtImageModelOptions as PixelArtImageProviderOptions,
} from './pixelart-image-model';
export type { PixelArtErrorData } from './pixelart-error';
export { VERSION } from './version';
```

Note: Following the existing convention, the `PixelArtImageProviderOptions` deprecated alias is included for consistency with other providers (xai, BFL).

---

### Test Files

#### File 13: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts`

Test the provider factory, following BFL's provider test pattern:

1. **Creates image models via `.image` and `.imageModel`** -- verify `provider`, `modelId`, `specificationVersion` properties.
2. **Configures baseURL and headers correctly** -- use `createTestServer`, verify the POST URL is `${baseURL}/images/generate`, verify `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header, verify custom headers are passed through, verify user agent includes `ai-sdk/pixelart/`.
3. **Throws NoSuchModelError for unsupported model types** -- test `languageModel()`, `embeddingModel()`.

#### File 14: `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts`

5. **Merges provider and request headers** -- verify `combineHeaders` behavior.
6. **Warns for unsupported features** -- aspectRatio, seed, mask, files.
7. **Handles API errors** -- 400 status with error body.

9. **Respects abort signal** -- abort controller test.

---

### Additional Files

#### File 15: `.changeset/add-pixelart-provider.md`

A changeset file for the new package (major, as per contributing guide for new packages):

### Files That Would Need Modification (documented, not implemented)

Per the requirements, no existing files should be modified. However, for completeness, the following would eventually need updates:

These are noted but NOT part of this implementation plan since the requirement states "do NOT modify any existing files/packages."

### Complete File Manifest

All files to create (13 new files in `packages/pixelart/`):

| 1 | `packages/pixelart/package.json` | Package manifest |

| 11 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts` | Provider factory + default instance |
| 12 | `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts` | Public API barrel exports |
| 13 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts` | Provider tests |
| 14 | `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts` | Image model tests |
| 15 | `.changeset/add-pixelart-provider.md` | Changeset for release |

---

### Dependency Graph

```
@ai-sdk/pixelart
  ├── @ai-sdk/provider (types: ImageModelV3, ProviderV3, NoSuchModelError, SharedV3Warning)
  ├── @ai-sdk/provider-utils (utils: loadApiKey, withoutTrailingSlash, withUserAgentSuffix,
  │    combineHeaders, postJsonToApi, createJsonResponseHandler,
  │    createJsonErrorResponseHandler, parseProviderOptions, lazySchema, zodSchema)
  ├── zod/v4 (z - for schemas)
  └── @ai-sdk/test-server (devDep, for tests)
```

### Key Design Rationale

1. **No polling**: The requirement specifies synchronous operation. The API returns images directly as base64 in the JSON response body, similar to xai and togetherai. This eliminates the entire polling infrastructure that BFL needs (pollIntervalMillis, pollTimeoutMillis, pollForImageUrl, delay, getFromApi for polling, bflPollSchema, bflSubmitSchema).

2. **`size` as standard option**: Unlike BFL which converts size to aspect ratio, and unlike xai which warns that size is unsupported, PixelArt passes `size` directly to the API body since the requirements specify it as a supported standard ImageModelV3 option.

3. **Provider options key `pixelArt`**: Following the camelCase convention (`blackForestLabs`, `togetherai`, `xai`).

4. **`lazySchema` + `zodSchema` wrapper**: Even though xai uses raw zod schemas, the BFL/togetherai pattern with `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(...))` is preferred for proper `InferSchema` type extraction and lazy evaluation. This is the newer/recommended pattern.

5. **`maxImagesPerCall = 4`**: Set as a static number per the requirements. Some providers use a function for dynamic limits, but a static number is simpler and sufficient here.

6. **Bearer auth**: Standard HTTP Bearer token auth unlike BFL's custom `x-key` header.

# Plan: Add `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Image Generation Provider

## Context

The task is to add a new fictional "PixelArt" AI provider to the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. This provider **only supports image generation** (no text, chat, or embeddings). It follows the established provider package structure, using `packages/black-forest-labs/` as the closest template (also image-only), but with a simpler synchronous (non-polling) API that returns base64 images directly (like the OpenAI image model pattern).

No existing files should be modified — only new files created.

---

## Files to Create (15 files)

### 1. Package Configuration (6 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/package.json`
- `"name": "@ai-sdk/pixelart"`, `"version": "0.0.0"` (per contributing guide)
- `"license": "Apache-2.0"`, `"sideEffects": false`
- Dependencies: `@ai-sdk/provider` (workspace:\*), `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace:\*)
- DevDependencies: `@ai-sdk/test-server` (workspace:\*), `@vercel/ai-tsconfig` (workspace:\*), `tsup` (^8), `typescript` (5.8.3), `zod` (3.25.76)
- PeerDependencies: `zod` (^3.25.76 || ^4.1.8)
- Exports: `.` → types/import/require pointing to `dist/`, plus `./package.json`
- Scripts: same as BFL (`build`, `clean`, `type-check`, `test`, `test:node`, `test:edge`, etc.)
- Remove BFL's `prepack`/`postpack` doc-copy scripts (no docs MDX to copy)
- Template: copy `packages/black-forest-labs/package.json`

#### `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json`
- Extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- `composite: true`, `rootDir: "src"`, `outDir: "dist"`
- References: `../provider`, `../provider-utils`, `../test-server`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.json`

#### `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json`
- Extends `./tsconfig.json`, overrides `composite: false`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.build.json`

#### `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts`
- Entry: `src/index.ts`, format: cjs + esm, dts: true, sourcemap: true
- Defines `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` from package.json version
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/tsup.config.ts`

#### `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js`
- Environment: `node`, include: `**/*.test.ts`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.node.config.js`

#### `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js`
- Environment: `edge-runtime`, include: `**/*.test.ts`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.edge.config.js`

---

### 2. Source Files (6 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts`
- Standard version constant with `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` build-time injection
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/src/version.ts`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`
- Export `PixelArtImageModelId` type: `'pixel-art-v1' | 'pixel-art-hd' | 'pixel-art-mini' | (string & {})`
- The `(string & {})` allows arbitrary model IDs while preserving autocomplete

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-error.ts`
- Define `pixelArtErrorDataSchema` using `z.object({ error: z.object({ message: z.string(), type: z.string().nullish(), code: z.union([z.string(), z.number()]).nullish() }) })`
- Export `PixelArtErrorData` type
- Export `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` using `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`
- Reuses: `createJsonErrorResponseHandler` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`, `z` from `zod/v4`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts`
Core implementation. Key details:

**Config interface:**
```typescript
interface PixelArtImageModelConfig {
  provider: string;
  baseURL: string;
  headers: Resolvable<Record<string, string | undefined>>;
  fetch?: FetchFunction;
  _internal?: { currentDate?: () => Date };
}
```

**Class `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3`:**
- `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- `maxImagesPerCall = 4`
- `provider` getter → `this.config.provider`
- Constructor: `(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId, config: PixelArtImageModelConfig)`

**`doGenerate()` method:**
1. Build warnings for unsupported features: `aspectRatio`, `seed`, `files`, `mask`
2. Parse provider options via `parseProviderOptions({ provider: 'pixelArt', providerOptions, schema: pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema })`
3. Build body: `{ model, prompt, n, size, style?, pixel_density? }`
4. POST to `${baseURL}/images/generate` via `postJsonToApi`
5. Use `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` for errors
6. Use `createJsonResponseHandler(pixelArtImageResponseSchema)` for success
7. Map `response.images[].b64_json` → return `images` array
8. Return `{ images, warnings, response: { timestamp, modelId, headers }, providerMetadata: { pixelArt: { created } } }`

**Response schema** (private, using `.nullish()` for API flexibility):
```typescript
const pixelArtImageResponseSchema = z.object({
  images: z.array(z.object({ b64_json: z.string() })),
  created: z.number().nullish(),
});
```

**Provider options schema** (exported):
```typescript
export const pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(z.object({
    style: z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional(),
    pixelDensity: z.number().min(1).max(100).optional(),
  })),
);
export type PixelArtImageModelOptions = InferSchema<typeof pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema>;
```

Reuses: `postJsonToApi`, `combineHeaders`, `resolve`, `createJsonResponseHandler`, `parseProviderOptions`, `lazySchema`, `zodSchema` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`; `ImageModelV3`, `SharedV3Warning` from `@ai-sdk/provider`; `z` from `zod/v4`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts`

**`PixelArtProviderSettings` interface:**
- `apiKey?: string` — defaults to `PIXELART_API_KEY` env var
- `baseURL?: string` — defaults to `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- `headers?: Record<string, string>` — custom headers
- `fetch?: FetchFunction` — custom fetch

**`PixelArtProvider` interface extends `ProviderV3`:**
- `image(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3`
- `imageModel(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3`
- `textEmbeddingModel(modelId: string): never` (deprecated alias)

**`createPixelArt(options?)` factory function:**
1. Resolve `baseURL` with `withoutTrailingSlash`, default `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
2. Build headers: `Authorization: Bearer ${loadApiKey({...})}`, spread `options.headers`, add user agent via `withUserAgentSuffix(headers, 'ai-sdk/pixelart/${VERSION}')`
3. `createImageModel(modelId)` → `new PixelArtImageModel(modelId, { provider: 'pixelart.image', baseURL, headers: getHeaders, fetch })`
4. Throw `NoSuchModelError` for `languageModel`, `embeddingModel`, `textEmbeddingModel`
5. Return `{ specificationVersion: 'v3', image, imageModel, languageModel, embeddingModel, textEmbeddingModel }`

**Default instance:** `export const pixelArt = createPixelArt();`

Reuses: `loadApiKey`, `withoutTrailingSlash`, `withUserAgentSuffix` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`; `NoSuchModelError`, `ProviderV3`, `ImageModelV3` from `@ai-sdk/provider`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts`
Barrel exports:
- `createPixelArt`, `pixelArt` (values)
- `PixelArtProvider`, `PixelArtProviderSettings` (types from provider)
- `PixelArtImageModelId` (type from settings)
- `PixelArtImageModelOptions` + deprecated alias `PixelArtImageProviderOptions` (types from model)
- `PixelArtErrorData` (type from error)
- `VERSION` (value from version)

---

### 3. Test Files (2 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts`
Uses `@ai-sdk/test-server/with-vitest` with `createTestServer`.

Test server endpoint: `https://api.example.com/v1/images/generate` returning `{ images: [{ b64_json: 'base64-image-data' }], created: 1700000000 }`.

**Test cases:**
1. **Constructor** — verify `provider`, `modelId`, `specificationVersion`, `maxImagesPerCall`
2. **Sends correct parameters** — prompt, n, size, model in request body
3. **Passes provider options** — `providerOptions: { pixelArt: { style: 'retro', pixelDensity: 50 } }` → body has `style: 'retro'`, `pixel_density: 50`
4. **Returns base64 images** — maps `images[].b64_json` correctly
5. **Merges provider and request headers** — verify Authorization + custom headers
6. **Warns for unsupported features** — aspectRatio, seed, files, mask each produce warnings
7. **Handles API errors** — 400 response with error body
8. **Response metadata** — timestamp, modelId, headers in response object
9. **Provider metadata** — `created` field in `providerMetadata.pixelArt`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts`
**Test cases:**
1. **Creates image models** — `.image()` and `.imageModel()` return models with correct provider/modelId
2. **Throws NoSuchModelError** — for `languageModel()`, `embeddingModel()`

---

### 4. Changeset (1 file)

#### `.changeset/add-pixelart-provider.md`
```
---
'@ai-sdk/pixelart': major
---

Add PixelArt image generation provider.
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|

# Plan: Add `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Image Generation Provider

## Context

The task is to add a new fictional "PixelArt" AI provider to the Vercel AI SDK monorepo. This provider **only supports image generation** (no text, chat, or embeddings). It follows the established provider package structure, using `packages/black-forest-labs/` as the closest template (also image-only), but with a simpler synchronous (non-polling) API that returns base64 images directly (like the OpenAI image model pattern).

No existing files should be modified — only new files created.

---

## Files to Create (15 files)

### 1. Package Configuration (6 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/package.json`
- `"name": "@ai-sdk/pixelart"`, `"version": "0.0.0"` (per contributing guide)
- `"license": "Apache-2.0"`, `"sideEffects": false`
- Dependencies: `@ai-sdk/provider` (workspace:\*), `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` (workspace:\*)
- DevDependencies: `@ai-sdk/test-server` (workspace:\*), `@vercel/ai-tsconfig` (workspace:\*), `tsup` (^8), `typescript` (5.8.3), `zod` (3.25.76)
- PeerDependencies: `zod` (^3.25.76 || ^4.1.8)
- Exports: `.` → types/import/require pointing to `dist/`, plus `./package.json`
- Scripts: same as BFL (`build`, `clean`, `type-check`, `test`, `test:node`, `test:edge`, etc.)
- Remove BFL's `prepack`/`postpack` doc-copy scripts (no docs MDX to copy)
- Template: copy `packages/black-forest-labs/package.json`

#### `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json`
- Extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- `composite: true`, `rootDir: "src"`, `outDir: "dist"`
- References: `../provider`, `../provider-utils`, `../test-server`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.json`

#### `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json`
- Extends `./tsconfig.json`, overrides `composite: false`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/tsconfig.build.json`

#### `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts`
- Entry: `src/index.ts`, format: cjs + esm, dts: true, sourcemap: true
- Defines `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` from package.json version
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/tsup.config.ts`

#### `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js`
- Environment: `node`, include: `**/*.test.ts`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.node.config.js`

#### `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js`
- Environment: `edge-runtime`, include: `**/*.test.ts`
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/vitest.edge.config.js`

---

### 2. Source Files (6 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts`
- Standard version constant with `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` build-time injection
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/src/version.ts`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`
- Export `PixelArtImageModelId` type: `'pixel-art-v1' | 'pixel-art-hd' | 'pixel-art-mini' | (string & {})`
- The `(string & {})` allows arbitrary model IDs while preserving autocomplete

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-error.ts`
- Define `pixelArtErrorDataSchema` using `z.object({ error: z.object({ message: z.string(), type: z.string().nullish(), code: z.union([z.string(), z.number()]).nullish() }) })`
- Export `PixelArtErrorData` type
- Export `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` using `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`
- Reuses: `createJsonErrorResponseHandler` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`, `z` from `zod/v4`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts`
Core implementation. Key details:

**Config interface:**
```typescript
interface PixelArtImageModelConfig {
  provider: string;
  baseURL: string;
  headers: Resolvable<Record<string, string | undefined>>;
  fetch?: FetchFunction;
  _internal?: { currentDate?: () => Date };
}
```

**Class `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3`:**
- `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- `maxImagesPerCall = 4`
- `provider` getter → `this.config.provider`
- Constructor: `(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId, config: PixelArtImageModelConfig)`

**`doGenerate()` method:**
1. Build warnings for unsupported features: `aspectRatio`, `seed`, `files`, `mask`
2. Parse provider options via `parseProviderOptions({ provider: 'pixelArt', providerOptions, schema: pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema })`
3. Build body: `{ model, prompt, n, size, style?, pixel_density? }`
4. POST to `${baseURL}/images/generate` via `postJsonToApi`
5. Use `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` for errors
6. Use `createJsonResponseHandler(pixelArtImageResponseSchema)` for success
7. Map `response.images[].b64_json` → return `images` array
8. Return `{ images, warnings, response: { timestamp, modelId, headers }, providerMetadata: { pixelArt: { created } } }`

**Response schema** (private, using `.nullish()` for API flexibility):
```typescript
const pixelArtImageResponseSchema = z.object({
  images: z.array(z.object({ b64_json: z.string() })),
  created: z.number().nullish(),
});
```

**Provider options schema** (exported):
```typescript
export const pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(z.object({
    style: z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional(),
    pixelDensity: z.number().min(1).max(100).optional(),
  })),
);
export type PixelArtImageModelOptions = InferSchema<typeof pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema>;
```

Reuses: `postJsonToApi`, `combineHeaders`, `resolve`, `createJsonResponseHandler`, `parseProviderOptions`, `lazySchema`, `zodSchema` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`; `ImageModelV3`, `SharedV3Warning` from `@ai-sdk/provider`; `z` from `zod/v4`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts`

**`PixelArtProviderSettings` interface:**
- `apiKey?: string` — defaults to `PIXELART_API_KEY` env var
- `baseURL?: string` — defaults to `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- `headers?: Record<string, string>` — custom headers
- `fetch?: FetchFunction` — custom fetch

**`PixelArtProvider` interface extends `ProviderV3`:**
- `image(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3`
- `imageModel(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3`
- `textEmbeddingModel(modelId: string): never` (deprecated alias)

**`createPixelArt(options?)` factory function:**
1. Resolve `baseURL` with `withoutTrailingSlash`, default `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
2. Build headers: `Authorization: Bearer ${loadApiKey({...})}`, spread `options.headers`, add user agent via `withUserAgentSuffix(headers, 'ai-sdk/pixelart/${VERSION}')`
3. `createImageModel(modelId)` → `new PixelArtImageModel(modelId, { provider: 'pixelart.image', baseURL, headers: getHeaders, fetch })`
4. Throw `NoSuchModelError` for `languageModel`, `embeddingModel`, `textEmbeddingModel`
5. Return `{ specificationVersion: 'v3', image, imageModel, languageModel, embeddingModel, textEmbeddingModel }`

**Default instance:** `export const pixelArt = createPixelArt();`

Reuses: `loadApiKey`, `withoutTrailingSlash`, `withUserAgentSuffix` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`; `NoSuchModelError`, `ProviderV3`, `ImageModelV3` from `@ai-sdk/provider`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts`
Barrel exports:
- `createPixelArt`, `pixelArt` (values)
- `PixelArtProvider`, `PixelArtProviderSettings` (types from provider)
- `PixelArtImageModelId` (type from settings)
- `PixelArtImageModelOptions` + deprecated alias `PixelArtImageProviderOptions` (types from model)
- `PixelArtErrorData` (type from error)
- `VERSION` (value from version)

---

### 3. Test Files (2 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts`
Uses `@ai-sdk/test-server/with-vitest` with `createTestServer`.

Test server endpoint: `https://api.example.com/v1/images/generate` returning `{ images: [{ b64_json: 'base64-image-data' }], created: 1700000000 }`.

**Test cases:**
1. **Constructor** — verify `provider`, `modelId`, `specificationVersion`, `maxImagesPerCall`
2. **Sends correct parameters** — prompt, n, size, model in request body
3. **Passes provider options** — `providerOptions: { pixelArt: { style: 'retro', pixelDensity: 50 } }` → body has `style: 'retro'`, `pixel_density: 50`
4. **Returns base64 images** — maps `images[].b64_json` correctly
5. **Merges provider and request headers** — verify Authorization + custom headers
6. **Warns for unsupported features** — aspectRatio, seed, files, mask each produce warnings
7. **Handles API errors** — 400 response with error body
8. **Response metadata** — timestamp, modelId, headers in response object
9. **Provider metadata** — `created` field in `providerMetadata.pixelArt`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts`
**Test cases:**
1. **Creates image models** — `.image()` and `.imageModel()` return models with correct provider/modelId
2. **Throws NoSuchModelError** — for `languageModel()`, `embeddingModel()`

---

### 4. Changeset (1 file)

#### `.changeset/add-pixelart-provider.md`
```
---
'@ai-sdk/pixelart': major
---

Add PixelArt image generation provider.
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| API pattern | Synchronous (direct base64 response) | Simpler than BFL polling; matches OpenAI image pattern |
| Auth header | `Authorization: Bearer <key>` | Standard pattern used by most providers |
| Provider options key | `pixelArt` (camelCase) | Convention: `blackForestLabs`, `togetherai`, `xai` |
| Options schema pattern | `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(...))` | Modern pattern for proper `InferSchema` type extraction |
| Provider options | `.optional()` | Per contributing guide: restrictive for future flexibility |
| Response schema | `.nullish()` | Per contributing guide: flexible for API changes |
| maxImagesPerCall | `4` (static) | Simple static number sufficient for this provider |
| Zod import | `import { z } from 'zod/v4'` | Project standard for new code |

---

## Verification

1. **Install dependencies**: `pnpm install` from workspace root
2. **Build**: `cd packages/pixelart && pnpm build` — verify dist/ output (index.js, index.mjs, index.d.ts)
3. **Type check**: `cd packages/pixelart && pnpm type-check`
4. **Tests**: `cd packages/pixelart && pnpm test` — runs both node and edge test suites
5. **Lint/format**: `pnpm fix` from root to auto-fix formatting
6. **Verify exports**: Check that `import { createPixelArt, pixelArt } from '@ai-sdk/pixelart'` resolves correctly

---

## Notes on Existing File Modifications (NOT in scope)

These would be needed for full integration but are explicitly excluded per requirements:
- `tsconfig.json` (root): add `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` to references
- Documentation: add `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/XX-pixelart.mdx`
- Examples: add `examples/ai-functions/src/generate-image/pixelart/basic.ts`

### 2. Source Files (6 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts`
- Standard version constant with `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` build-time injection
- Identical to `packages/black-forest-labs/src/version.ts`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts`
- Export `PixelArtImageModelId` type: `'pixel-art-v1' | 'pixel-art-hd' | 'pixel-art-mini' | (string & {})`
- The `(string & {})` allows arbitrary model IDs while preserving autocomplete

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-error.ts`
- Define `pixelArtErrorDataSchema` using `z.object({ error: z.object({ message: z.string(), type: z.string().nullish(), code: z.union([z.string(), z.number()]).nullish() }) })`
- Export `PixelArtErrorData` type
- Export `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` using `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`
- Reuses: `createJsonErrorResponseHandler` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`, `z` from `zod/v4`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts`
Core implementation. Key details:

**Config interface:**
```typescript
interface PixelArtImageModelConfig {
  provider: string;
  baseURL: string;
  headers: Resolvable<Record<string, string | undefined>>;
  fetch?: FetchFunction;
  _internal?: { currentDate?: () => Date };
}
```

**Class `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3`:**
- `specificationVersion = 'v3'`
- `maxImagesPerCall = 4`
- `provider` getter → `this.config.provider`
- Constructor: `(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId, config: PixelArtImageModelConfig)`

**`doGenerate()` method:**
1. Build warnings for unsupported features: `aspectRatio`, `seed`, `files`, `mask`
2. Parse provider options via `parseProviderOptions({ provider: 'pixelArt', providerOptions, schema: pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema })`
3. Build body: `{ model, prompt, n, size, style?, pixel_density? }`
4. POST to `${baseURL}/images/generate` via `postJsonToApi`
5. Use `pixelArtFailedResponseHandler` for errors
6. Use `createJsonResponseHandler(pixelArtImageResponseSchema)` for success
7. Map `response.images[].b64_json` → return `images` array
8. Return `{ images, warnings, response: { timestamp, modelId, headers }, providerMetadata: { pixelArt: { created } } }`

**Response schema** (private, using `.nullish()` for API flexibility):
```typescript
const pixelArtImageResponseSchema = z.object({
  images: z.array(z.object({ b64_json: z.string() })),
  created: z.number().nullish(),
});
```

**Provider options schema** (exported):
```typescript
export const pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(z.object({
    style: z.enum(['pixel', 'retro', '8bit']).optional(),
    pixelDensity: z.number().min(1).max(100).optional(),
  })),
);
export type PixelArtImageModelOptions = InferSchema<typeof pixelArtImageModelOptionsSchema>;
```

Reuses: `postJsonToApi`, `combineHeaders`, `resolve`, `createJsonResponseHandler`, `parseProviderOptions`, `lazySchema`, `zodSchema` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`; `ImageModelV3`, `SharedV3Warning` from `@ai-sdk/provider`; `z` from `zod/v4`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts`

**`PixelArtProviderSettings` interface:**
- `apiKey?: string` — defaults to `PIXELART_API_KEY` env var
- `baseURL?: string` — defaults to `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- `headers?: Record<string, string>` — custom headers
- `fetch?: FetchFunction` — custom fetch

**`PixelArtProvider` interface extends `ProviderV3`:**
- `image(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3`
- `imageModel(modelId: PixelArtImageModelId): ImageModelV3`
- `textEmbeddingModel(modelId: string): never` (deprecated alias)

**`createPixelArt(options?)` factory function:**
1. Resolve `baseURL` with `withoutTrailingSlash`, default `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
2. Build headers: `Authorization: Bearer ${loadApiKey({...})}`, spread `options.headers`, add user agent via `withUserAgentSuffix(headers, 'ai-sdk/pixelart/${VERSION}')`
3. `createImageModel(modelId)` → `new PixelArtImageModel(modelId, { provider: 'pixelart.image', baseURL, headers: getHeaders, fetch })`
4. Throw `NoSuchModelError` for `languageModel`, `embeddingModel`, `textEmbeddingModel`
5. Return `{ specificationVersion: 'v3', image, imageModel, languageModel, embeddingModel, textEmbeddingModel }`

**Default instance:** `export const pixelArt = createPixelArt();`

Reuses: `loadApiKey`, `withoutTrailingSlash`, `withUserAgentSuffix` from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`; `NoSuchModelError`, `ProviderV3`, `ImageModelV3` from `@ai-sdk/provider`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts`
Barrel exports:
- `createPixelArt`, `pixelArt` (values)
- `PixelArtProvider`, `PixelArtProviderSettings` (types from provider)
- `PixelArtImageModelId` (type from settings)
- `PixelArtImageModelOptions` + deprecated alias `PixelArtImageProviderOptions` (types from model)
- `PixelArtErrorData` (type from error)
- `VERSION` (value from version)

### 3. Test Files (2 files)

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts`
Uses `@ai-sdk/test-server/with-vitest` with `createTestServer`.

Test server endpoint: `https://api.example.com/v1/images/generate` returning `{ images: [{ b64_json: 'base64-image-data' }], created: 1700000000 }`.

**Test cases:**
1. **Constructor** — verify `provider`, `modelId`, `specificationVersion`, `maxImagesPerCall`
2. **Sends correct parameters** — prompt, n, size, model in request body
3. **Passes provider options** — `providerOptions: { pixelArt: { style: 'retro', pixelDensity: 50 } }` → body has `style: 'retro'`, `pixel_density: 50`
4. **Returns base64 images** — maps `images[].b64_json` correctly
5. **Merges provider and request headers** — verify Authorization + custom headers
6. **Warns for unsupported features** — aspectRatio, seed, files, mask each produce warnings
7. **Handles API errors** — 400 response with error body
8. **Response metadata** — timestamp, modelId, headers in response object
9. **Provider metadata** — `created` field in `providerMetadata.pixelArt`

#### `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts`
**Test cases:**
1. **Creates image models** — `.image()` and `.imageModel()` return models with correct provider/modelId
2. **Throws NoSuchModelError** — for `languageModel()`, `embeddingModel()`

### 4. Changeset (1 file)

#### `.changeset/add-pixelart-provider.md`
```
---
'@ai-sdk/pixelart': major

Add PixelArt image generation provider.
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| API pattern | Synchronous (direct base64 response) | Simpler than BFL polling; matches OpenAI image pattern |
| Auth header | `Authorization: Bearer <key>` | Standard pattern used by most providers |
| Provider options key | `pixelArt` (camelCase) | Convention: `blackForestLabs`, `togetherai`, `xai` |
| Options schema pattern | `lazySchema(() => zodSchema(...))` | Modern pattern for proper `InferSchema` type extraction |
| Provider options | `.optional()` | Per contributing guide: restrictive for future flexibility |
| Response schema | `.nullish()` | Per contributing guide: flexible for API changes |
| maxImagesPerCall | `4` (static) | Simple static number sufficient for this provider |
| Zod import | `import { z } from 'zod/v4'` | Project standard for new code |

---

## Verification

1. **Install dependencies**: `pnpm install` from workspace root
2. **Build**: `cd packages/pixelart && pnpm build` — verify dist/ output (index.js, index.mjs, index.d.ts)
3. **Type check**: `cd packages/pixelart && pnpm type-check`
4. **Tests**: `cd packages/pixelart && pnpm test` — runs both node and edge test suites
5. **Lint/format**: `pnpm fix` from root to auto-fix formatting
6. **Verify exports**: Check that `import { createPixelArt, pixelArt } from '@ai-sdk/pixelart'` resolves correctly

---

## Notes on Existing File Modifications (NOT in scope)

These would be needed for full integration but are explicitly excluded per requirements:
- `tsconfig.json` (root): add `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` to references
- Documentation: add `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/XX-pixelart.mdx`
- Examples: add `examples/ai-functions/src/generate-image/pixelart/basic.ts`

7	  string,
8	  {
9	    images: JSONArray;
10	  } & JSONValue
11	>;
12	
13	type GetMaxImagesPerCallFunction = (options: {
14	  modelId: string;
15	}) => PromiseLike<number | undefined> | number | undefined;
16	
17	/**
18	 * Image generation model specification version 3.
19	 */
20	export type ImageModelV3 = {
21	  /**
22	   * The image model must specify which image model interface
23	   * version it implements. This will allow us to evolve the image
24	   * model interface and retain backwards compatibility. The different
25	   * implementation versions can be handled as a discriminated union
26	   * on our side.
27	   */
28	  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3';
29	
30	  /**
31	   * Name of the provider for logging purposes.
32	   */
33	  readonly provider: string;
34	
35	  /**
36	   * Provider-specific model ID for logging purposes.
37	   */
38	  readonly modelId: string;
39	
40	  /**
41	   * Limit of how many images can be generated in a single API call.
42	   * Can be set to a number for a fixed limit, to undefined to use
43	   * the global limit, or a function that returns a number or undefined,
44	   * optionally as a promise.
45	   */
46	  readonly maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | GetMaxImagesPerCallFunction;
47	
48	  /**
49	   * Generates an array of images.
50	   */
51	  doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions): PromiseLike<{
52	    /**
53	     * Generated images as base64 encoded strings or binary data.
54	     * The images should be returned without any unnecessary conversion.
55	     * If the API returns base64 encoded strings, the images should be returned
56	     * as base64 encoded strings. If the API returns binary data, the images should
57	     * be returned as binary data.
58	     */
59	    images: Array<string> | Array<Uint8Array>;
60	
61	    /**
62	     * Warnings for the call, e.g. unsupported features.
63	     */
64	    warnings: Array<SharedV3Warning>;
65	
66	    /**
67	     * Additional provider-specific metadata. They are passed through
68	     * from the provider to the AI SDK and enable provider-specific
69	     * results that can be fully encapsulated in the provider.
70	     *
71	     * The outer record is keyed by the provider name, and the inner
72	     * record is provider-specific metadata. It always includes an
73	     * `images` key with image-specific metadata
74	     *
75	     * ```ts
76	     * {
77	     * "openai": {
78	     * "images": ["revisedPrompt": "Revised prompt here."]
79	     * }
80	     * }
81	     * ```
82	     */
83	    providerMetadata?: ImageModelV3ProviderMetadata;
84	
85	    /**
86	     * Response information for telemetry and debugging purposes.
87	     */
88	    response: {
89	      /**
90	       * Timestamp for the start of the generated response.
91	       */
92	      timestamp: Date;
93	
94	      /**
95	       * The ID of the response model that was used to generate the response.
96	       */
97	      modelId: string;
98	
99	      /**
100	       * Response headers.
101	       */
102	      headers: Record<string, string> | undefined;
103	    };
104	
105	    /**
106	     * Optional token usage for the image generation call (if the provider reports it).
107	     */
108	    usage?: ImageModelV3Usage;
109	  }>;
110	};

5	  /**

7	   */
8	  prompt: string | undefined;
9	
10	  /**
11	   * Number of images to generate.
12	   */
13	  n: number;
14	
15	  /**

18	   * `undefined` will use the provider's default size.
19	   */
20	  size: `${number}x${number}` | undefined;
21	
22	  /**
23	   * Aspect ratio of the images to generate.
24	   * Must have the format `{width}:{height}`.
25	   * `undefined` will use the provider's default aspect ratio.
26	   */
27	  aspectRatio: `${number}:${number}` | undefined;
28	
29	  /**
30	   * Seed for the image generation.
31	   * `undefined` will use the provider's default seed.
32	   */
33	  seed: number | undefined;
34	
35	  /**
36	   * Array of images for image editing or variation generation.
37	   * The images should be provided as base64 encoded strings or binary data.
38	   */
39	  files: ImageModelV3File[] | undefined;
40	
41	  /**
42	   * Mask image for inpainting operations.
43	   * The mask should be provided as base64 encoded strings or binary data.
44	   */
45	  mask: ImageModelV3File | undefined;
46	
47	  /**
48	   * Additional provider-specific options that are passed through to the provider
49	   * as body parameters.
50	   *
51	   * The outer record is keyed by the provider name, and the inner
52	   * record is keyed by the provider-specific metadata key.
53	   *
54	   * ```ts
55	   * {

63

68	
69	  /**

71	   * Only applicable for HTTP-based providers.
72	   */
73	  headers?: Record<string, string | undefined>;
74	};

1	# AI SDK - OpenAI Provider
2	
3	The **[OpenAI provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/openai)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs)
4	contains language model support for the OpenAI chat and completion APIs and embedding model support for the OpenAI embeddings API.
5	
6	## Setup
7	
8	The OpenAI provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/openai` module. You can install it with
9	
10	```bash
11	npm i @ai-sdk/openai
12	```
13	
14	## Skill for Coding Agents
15	
16	If you use coding agents such as Claude Code or Cursor, we highly recommend adding the AI SDK skill to your repository:
17	
18	```shell
19	npx skills add vercel/ai
20	```
21	
22	## Provider Instance
23	
24	You can import the default provider instance `openai` from `@ai-sdk/openai`:
25	
26	```ts
27	import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';
28	```
29	
30	## Example
31	
32	```ts
33	import { openai } from '@ai-sdk/openai';
34	import { generateText } from 'ai';
35	
36	const { text } = await generateText({
37	  model: openai('gpt-5-mini'),
38	  prompt: 'Write a vegetarian lasagna recipe for 4 people.',
39	});
40	```
41	
42	## Documentation
43	
44	Please check out the **[OpenAI provider documentation](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/openai)** for more information.
45

content.text:
Everything is in place. Here's a summary of everything that was created:

## New `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Provider Package

### Files Created (13 new files, 1 modified):

**Package configuration:**
- `packages/pixelart/package.json` — Package definition with workspace dependencies on `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`
- `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json` — TypeScript config extending the shared `ts-library.json`
- `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json` — Build-specific TS config (disables composite)
- `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts` — Build config for CJS + ESM output with version injection
- `packages/pixelart/turbo.json` — Turborepo task config
- `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js` — Node test runner config
- `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js` — Edge runtime test runner config

**Source files:**
- `packages/pixelart/src/index.ts` — Public exports: `createPixelArt`, `pixelart`, types
- `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.ts` — `createPixelArt()` factory function and `PixelArtProvider` interface. Only exposes `image()`/`imageModel()` — throws `NoSuchModelError` for `languageModel` and `embeddingModel`. Base URL defaults to `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`
- `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.ts` — `PixelArtImageModel` implementing `ImageModelV3`. Supports `size`, `style` ("pixel", "retro", "8bit"), and `pixelDensity` (1–64) via provider options
- `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-settings.ts` — Model ID type (`pixelart-v1`, `pixelart-v2`, `pixelart-v2-hd`, or custom string)
- `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-error.ts` — Error response handler
- `packages/pixelart/src/version.ts` — Build-time version injection

**Test files:**
- `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-provider.test.ts` — 7 tests for provider creation and model type restrictions
- `packages/pixelart/src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts` — 12 tests for image generation, headers, settings, provider options, warnings

**Modified:**
- `tsconfig.json` (root) — Added `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` reference in alphabetical order

### Verification:
- ✅ **19 tests passing** (`pnpm test:node`)
- ✅ **Type-check clean** (`pnpm type-check`)
- ✅ No existing provider packages were modified
